companyDescription:
YouLend is the leading global embedded financing platform, powering e-commerce, payments, and tech giants like Amazon, eBay, Dojo, Mollie, Shopify, Just Eat Takeaway, and SumUp.

Our software platform and APIs enable our partners to offer flexible financing, in their own branding, within a week.

Operating across 9+ markets in the UK, EU, and US, we help businesses grow. Learn more at www.youlend.com or connect with our team at partnership@youlend.com.
companySlogan: The global leader in embedded SME finance.
descriptionHtml:
About Us

YouLend is the preferred global embedded financing platform for many of the world's leading e-commerce sites, tech companies and payment services providers such as Amazon, eBay, Shopify, Mollie, Dojo, Paysafe, Just Eat Takeaway and Takepayments. Our software platform and APIs enable our partners to offer flexible financing products, in their desired branding, to their merchant base. With YouLend's AI-driven credit risk assessment solutions, more merchants and SMEs than ever can receive fast, flexible and affordable funding. We operate in 9+ geographies across the UK, EU and the US.

As a UK-licensed Payment Institution (PI) and in the final stages of securing EU PI authorization, we are committed to ensuring a best-in-class regulatory compliance framework that supports our growth while meeting evolving regulatory expectations.

We believe that the future of financial services will be delivered by customer-oriented tech companies that embed financing in their customer journeys, and we are building the solutions that will power that future.      

The Role

The Regulatory Compliance Manager will be a key figure in ensuring YouLend maintains a robust regulatory compliance framework. Reporting directly to the Head of Regulatory Compliance this role will focus on enhancing compliance processes, monitoring regulatory risks, and ensuring adherence to FCA, EU, and global standards as we pursue additional regulatory authorisations.

This role offers an exciting opportunity for an experienced compliance professional to play a pivotal role in helping to shape our regulatory compliance framework and support YouLend with future expansion plans.

Requirements

  • Regulatory Compliance Framework - Contribute to the maturity of the regulatory compliance strategy and framework, ensuring there is a consistent and scalable approach to ‘compliance' across training, policies, KRIs, testing, reporting.
  • Compliance Monitoring - Support with the enhancement of the design and implementation of YouLend's Compliance Monitoring and Testing Programme including tracking and monitoring progress against the annual monitoring program and adapting it to the changing regulatory and business environment.
  • Governance and Management Reporting - Raising the standards of management reporting and governance, assisting the Head of Regulatory Compliance with preparing updates to YouLend's Senior Management Team where necessary and embedding a governance framework that facilitates this.
  • Horizon scanning - Through the identification, assessment, and ongoing monitoring of current and future regulatory risks, including gap analysis of new regulatory initiatives and guidance from the Youlend's regulators
  • Advisory - Act as a trusted advisor to the business, providing guidance on regulatory topics and helping the business navigate complex compliance landscapes in the UK, EU, US and other jurisdictions.
  • Regulatory Engagement - Support the Head of Regulatory Compliance with managing regulatory engagement, tracking and handling queries, audits, and ongoing communications with YouLend's Regulators.
  • Regulatory Reporting - Lead regulatory reporting processes to ensure timely and accurate submissions to the FCA and other relevant authorities.
  • Enterprise Risk Management - Support with the uplift of YouLend's Enterprise Risk Framework, working with risk owners across the business to develop and mature the identification, assessment and management of YouLend's enterprise risks.

Essential Qualifications And Experience

  • 3- 5 years' experience in a regulatory compliance role, ideally within a payment institution, electronic money institution or a similar regulated entity.
  • Strong analytical skills with the ability to identify and solve complex business problems
  • Demonstrable experience of supporting the implementation of compliance frameworks, particularly for FCA-regulated entities.
  • Experience leading regulatory reporting processes.
  • Familiarity with EU regulatory regimes and experience supporting applications for new regulatory authorizations is a strong plus.
  • Experience working in B2B financing or corporate lending is desirable.
  • Experience applying statistical techniques and analytical methods to extract insights from data.

Skills

  • Strong knowledge of FCA regulatory requirements, including the FCA Handbook and Payment Services Regulations (PSRs). 
  • Proven ability to assess and manage regulatory risks, with experience in conducting thematic reviews and compliance monitoring.
  • Expertise in key compliance themes such as governance, complaints handling, safeguarding, product governance and outsourcing, and in depth understanding of risk mitigation frameworks.
  • Exceptional analytical skills, including conducting gap analyses to address new regulatory initiatives and guidance.
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills, with experience preparing high-quality reviews, assessments, and reports for senior stakeholders and regulators.

Benefits

Why join YouLend?

  •  Award-Winning Workplace: YouLend has been recognised as one of the "Best Places to Work 2024 and 2025" by the Sunday Times for being a supportive, diverse, and rewarding workplace.
  •  Award-Winning Fintech: YouLend has been recognised as a "Top 250 Fintech Worldwide" company by CNBC.

We offer comprehensive benefits package that includes:

  • Stock Options
  • Private Medical insurance via Vitality and Bupa Dental
  • EAP with Health Assured
  • Enhanced Maternity and Paternity Leave
  • Modern and sophisticated office space in Central London
  • Free Gym in office building in Holborn
  • Subsidised Lunch via Feedr
  • Deliveroo Allowance if working late in office
  • Monthly in office Masseuse
  • Team and Company Socials
  • Football Power League

At YouLend, we champion diversity and embrace equal opportunity employment practices. Our hiring, transfer, and promotion decisions are exclusively based on qualifications, merit, and business requirements, free from any discrimination based on race, gender, age, disability, religion, nationality, or any other protected basis under applicable law.

companyDescription:
Kovai.co is an AI-first software company offering multiple products at scale in the B2B SaaS space. We provide smart, future-ready solutions for small, medium, and enterprise organizations across industries.  
The organization offers a wide range of business software products. From robust enterprise software for Microsoft BizTalk and Azure Serverless platforms to Knowledge Management SaaS, we cover it all.
Our product team consists of thinkers and innovators who are redefining the way robust Enterprise Software and SaaS products are built. Headquartered in London, U.K., and with a development center in Coimbatore, India, our engineers have niche skills, in-depth domain knowledge, and a strong focus on AI-driven capabilities

descriptionHtml:
We are looking for an amazing New Business Account Executive for our Document360 team at Kovai. They will join our London Bridge office and will be key to driving revenue from the UK. This account executive will be responsible for finding and prospecting their own leads, but they will also get a dedicated SDR who will source exclusively for them.


Document360 is part of Kovai. Kovai is a catalyst, sparking a revolution in the world of enterprise software and B2B SaaS. We are a technology powerhouse that delivers best-in-class enterprise software and game-changing SaaS solutions across industries.


At Kovai, we're rewriting the B2B landscape by empowering over 2,500 businesses worldwide with our award-winning SaaS solutions.


Our Products:



  • Document360

  • Turbo360

  • Biztalk360


“UK headquarters. Indian innovation. Global impact.”


Our journey has been nothing short of remarkable, having witnessed exponential growth and profitability right from our inception.


We are on track towards $30 million in annual revenue – and we're just getting started.


Document360:


Document360 is the fastest-growing product in the Kovai portfolio. It is an AI-powered knowledge base platform for customers and teams. It is a self-service platform for building, sharing, and managing knowledge bases, product documentation, manuals, SOPs, Wikis, and more.


We are trusted by global brands like Gong, Rapid7, Airtable, McDonalds, VMware, Virgin, NHS, Whatfix, Payoneer, etc.


Document360 has made an impact across teams and industries-


1. The productivity of employees increased by 40%


2. Support tickets have been reduced by 30%


3. Website visits increased by 50%


Tasks

Sales and Revenue Generation:




  • Achieve sales targets by closing outbound accounts from Mid-Market to Enterprise Customers



  • Get meetings with target customers independently as well as in conjunction with your SDR



  • Hitting Revenue targets consistently



Strategic Account Planning:




  • Develop and implement strategic account plans to meet sales objectives.



  • Collaborate with internal teams to align products or services with client needs.



Communication and Presentation:




  • Effectively communicate the value proposition of products or services to clients.



  • Conduct presentations, product demonstrations, and sales pitches.



Negotiation and Contract Management:




  • Negotiate terms and conditions with clients to secure agreements.



  • Manage contract negotiations and ensure compliance with company policies.



Forecasting and Reporting:




  • Provide accurate sales forecasts and regular reports on account activities.



  • Analyze sales data and market trends to identify opportunities and challenges.



Customer Service and Issue Resolution:




  • Address client inquiries, concerns, and issues in a timely and effective manner.



  • Collaborate with customer support teams to ensure client satisfaction.



Market and Competitor Analysis:




  • Stay informed about industry trends, market conditions, and competitor activities.Use market knowledge to position products or services effectively.



Requirements


  • 2+ years of proven ability in managing complex 3-9 month SaaS sales cycles with a track record of acquiring new accounts and successful revenue attainment.




  • Ability to be in the London Bridge Office 2-3 days a week




  • Experience in solution-based and consultative sales.




  • Skilled at negotiating business terms with line-of-business, procurement and contract teams, senior management and/or C-level executives.




  • Proven ability to influence & drive cross-departmental strategy and collaboration (Support, Product,, Senior Leadership) during deal cycles in creatively solving our client’s business problems.




  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills and excels in leading web-based demos.




  • Process and solution-oriented, with a customer mindset and the ability to challenge the status quo, I uncover and prove multiple land and expand use cases.




  • Experience in the knowledge base industry is not required, but understanding the opportunity in the market is.




  • Quarterly Team Days (Mini Golf and Cricket were the last two!)




  • 25 Days Annual Leave




  • Opportunities for International Travel




  • Competitive Software Salary (Base + Commission = 120k+ Total)




  • Life Insurance cover




  • Fresh Laptop on Arrival

companyDescription:
Established in 2010, we are an accountancy and finance recruitment consultancy with offices in London and Reading.

We are experts in accountancy, and our consultants are industry specialists across Industry & Commerce, Financial Services and Not-for-Profit.

We have the capability to deliver traditional permanent recruitment, interim and contract assignments, or executive search and multi-vacancy retainers.

We are market leaders with a widely experienced leadership team and carefully trained and developed consultants at every level.

We have always been passionate about assessment, and providing our clients with great candidates and candidates with roles they will thrive in. Back in the day this was coined as ‘3 layers of fit’. 

We want to reduce the likelihood of a miss-hire by providing airtight shortlists with three aspects in mind:

1) Are they competent?

2) Do they have the right qualifications and skills?

3) Will they culturally add?

Our experience, our competency framework and bespoke profiling tool PFIT™ means the level of insight we provide clients at shortlist can be far beyond our competition. 

All in all, saving you a lot of time, so you can do the stuff you are meant to be doing.

If you’d like to learn how we can help, please feel free to pick up the telephone for a confidential conversation.

You can get in touch with our London Team on 020 7042 8420 or our South East Team on 01189 077 920.

We look forward to hearing from you.

Finance Associate, Investment Management firm, London, Hybrid, up to £67k

We will be reviewing CVs throughout the process so please apply as soon as you are able to.


We are committed to ensuring the entire process is inclusive and accessible for all candidates that fit the minimum requirements for our role. As such, we encourage applications from under-represented groups in the accountancy sector. Please let us know if we can make any reasonable adjustments for you at any stage in the process.


A global Investment Manager are looking to welcome a Finance Associate into their finance team. This person will report into the Finance Manager and be responsible monthly accounting, preparing management reports and annual financial statements.


This is a great opportunity for a newly qualified ACA in audit looking to make their first move into industry. They are looking to offer the successful candidate between £64,000 - £67,000.

Key Responsibilities

•Own month-end close, journal entries, and reconciliations

•Prepare management packs with variance analysis and commentary

•Partner with teams to support budgeting and commercial decisions

•Ensure compliance with financial reporting, audit, and tax obligations

•Assist with cash flow forecasting and funding needs

•Drive process improvements and maintain strong internal controls

•Support cross-functional finance activities and ad hoc projects


What We’re Looking For

•ACA qualified

•Strong grasp of FRS 102 and financial control environments

•Excellent problem-solving, organisation, and communication skills

•Detail-oriented, proactive, and commercially aware

•A collaborative team player with a growth mindset


At Trace, we are committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse workplace and believe that, in any business, it is imperative that all members of staff feel appreciated and respected in their roles. Our job is simply to find the best candidate on the market, regardless of their age, race, religion, disability, gender identity, sexual orientation or any other factor irrelevant to their qualifications and competencies.

If you have any further questions regarding accessibility throughout the process, then please get in touch DEIB@tracerecruit.com or call a member of our DEIB Champions team.

descriptionHtml:
About Us

Re7 Capital is a London-based cryptoasset investment firm. We operate a range of innovative investment strategies at the frontier of digital assets, supported by proprietary data infrastructure and deep domain expertise. Our team combines experience in institutional finance, crypto-native markets, and advanced analytics.

We're hiring a Fund Operations Manager to support the continued growth of our fund platform. This role spans fund operations, legal and compliance, reporting, and vendor management. You'll work closely with portfolio management, fund administrators, and internal leadership to ensure Re7 operates with precision and scalability as we expand globally.

Responsibilities

Fund Operations & Reporting

  • Oversee the client onboarding process for new investors
  • Manage the fund valuation process in collaboration with fund administrators and portfolio teams
  • Liaise with administrators and internal teams to ensure timely, accurate investor reporting
  • Identify and implement process improvements via tooling, automation, or procedural upgrades
  • Support ad hoc operational needs across the business when capacity is needed

Legal, Compliance & Regulatory Oversight

  • Manage fund licensing requirements across multiple jurisdictions
  • Ensure all sales and marketing activities comply with applicable regulatory restrictions
  • Build business cases and assess feasibility of expanding regulatory licensing where relevant
  • Oversee the creation and management of legal entities in support of new markets or product launches
  • Draft, review, and negotiate legal agreements with vendors, clients, and partners

Vendor & Relationship Management

  • Maintain oversight of key external service providers (e.g. administrators, legal counsel, tech vendors)
  • Ensure service standards align with contractual obligations and internal policy
  • Monitor vendor performance and support efforts to maximise value-for-money

Strategic & Cross-Functional Support

  • Contribute to new business and product expansion initiatives
  • Step in to support other areas of the business as needed during periods of high activity or staff leave

Requirements

  • 5+ years of operations experience across Crypto, TradFi and/or FinTech, at least 2 of those in Investment Fund Operations or Fund Administration
  • Legal training, experience and/or qualification
  • Ability to quickly convert strategy into actions and execution
  • Excellent communication skills, both written and ability to influence across a variety of business areas & seniorities
  • Demonstrated success in change management
  • Highly organised and adaptable to changing requirements and priorities
  • Strong work ethic and results focused, accountable for consistent results
  • Self-starter with the ability to work independently in a fast-paced environment with a high degree of ownership

Attributes

  • Ownership mentality and strong accountability
  • Structured, process-driven approach with a mindset for continuous improvement
  • High integrity and strong communication skills
  • Calm under pressure, able to work cross-functionally and independently
  • Pragmatic, dependable, and solutions-oriented

Benefits

  • Remote-first structure with opportunities for in-person collaboration
  • Collaborative, high-performance culture
  • Exposure to cutting-edge crypto and digital asset strategies
  • Competitive compensation package
  • UK visa sponsorship available for exceptional candidates

How To Apply

Please send your CV along with a short answer to the following question:

Describe a time you identified and implemented an operational improvement that saved time, reduced risk and improved accuracy

(200 words max. Please include: what the issue was, how you spotted it, what action you took, and what the measurable outcome was)

Unily’s comprehensive platform is redefining employee experiences for the modern workplace, equipping enterprises with the tools to maximize organizational velocity, while enabling better alignment, engagement, and empowerment across distributed teams.
companySlogan: #1 AI-powered Employee Experience Platform driving organizational velocity for the world's most iconic brands.
descriptionHtml:
About Unily

Unily partners with the world’s largest and most complex enterprises to power Organizational Velocity through digital Employee Experience transformation. Iconic brands, including Estée Lauder Companies, CVS Health, and British Airways, use Unily’s market-leading Employee Experience platform to improve productivity, streamline communication, and foster a highly connected workplace.


Unily is the only triple leader recognized by all three of the major analysts. Unily is recognized as a Leader in the 2024 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Intranet Packaged Solutions, the 2024 Forrester Wave™: Intranet Platforms, and the IDC MarketScape: Worldwide Experience-Centric Intelligent Digital Workspaces 2024. With these accolades, we continue to grow and expand our employee community with people who are passionate about joining us on this exciting journey.


Job Purpose

We’re looking for a proactive, organised and detail-oriented People Assistant to join our People Team. With around 200 employees in the UK and more growth ahead, this is a broad, hands-on generalist role that touches every part of the employee experience – ideal for someone who enjoys variety, is comfortable with data and systems, wants to learn fast, and build strong foundations in HR.


You’ll be involved in HR administration, systems, data analysis, benefits, compliance and reporting – helping to keep everything running smoothly. You’ll also support cross-functional projects, learning and development, DE&I initiatives, and internal events. This is a great opportunity for someone early in their HR career who’s keen to learn fast, develop depth across HR fundamentals, and grow in a modern, scaling SaaS business.


We’re looking for someone who’s tech-savvy, process-minded, and curious - whether that’s improving how we run onboarding, streamlining admin tasks, or turning people data into useful insights. You’re the kind of person who spots when a spreadsheet is messy, a process could be clearer, or a dashboard could be automated - and you want to make it better. If you enjoy asking “why do we do it this way?” - and helping us improve it - you’ll thrive here.


This is a hybrid, based in one of our offices, London or Eashing, Surrey, with occasional travel to both offices.


Main Responsibilities:

People Operations

  • Manage end-to-end employee lifecycle admin: offer letters, contracts, changes, probation tracking, offboarding, references, and leaver documentation
  • Ensure all employee records are centralised, accurate, and kept up to date across systems (e.g. HRIS, payroll, benefits platforms)
  • Act as the first point of contact for day-to-day employee queries on policies, benefits, systems and processes
  • Take minutes during ER meetings (e.g. disciplinary, performance, grievances, redundancies)
  • Help track key lifecycle moments (probation, parental leave, returns, etc.) and trigger the necessary actions
  • Coordinate onboarding logistics in collaboration with Talent Acquisition, IT, and Office teams - including systems setup, and induction schedules
  • Support internal and external audits by gathering accurate documentation and responding to data requests


Data, Reporting & Insights

  • Prepare monthly and ad hoc people reports (headcount, turnover, DEI, leavers, engagement) and dashboards
  • Conduct data analysis to identify patterns, gaps, or insights to help drive People priorities
  • Support quarterly Talent Committee meetings with timely and accurate data and reporting packs
  • Support reporting for external purposes (e.g. gender pay gap, ESG, insurance, board packs)
  • Suggest ways to improve how we collect, analyse, and visualise people data


Payroll & Benefits

  • Maintain payroll input files: starters, leavers, changes etc.
  • Keep benefits enrolment, changes, and leaver data up to date with providers
  • Liaise with providers to resolve benefit-related queries and issues (pensions, private healthcare, insurances, etc.)
  • Track eligibility and enrolment timelines (e.g. pensions, private healthcare)
  • Maintain up-to-date benefits collateral and documentation for employees
  • Help coordinate annual benefits review and communications
  • Deliver benefits inductions to new joiners, ensuring clear understanding of options and enrolment processes


Systems, Processes & Improvements

  • Take ownership of day-to-day admin processes, with a mindset of continuous improvement
  • Maintain and improve our HRIS (Rippling), ensuring workflows are accurate, up-to-date, and scalable as we grow
  • Identify pain points in admin processes and help simplify, automate, or digitise them
  • Build and maintain internal documentation to make processes clear, repeatable, and scalable
  • Support wider People initiatives by contributing to process launches or system rollouts


Culture, Projects & Learning

  • Help improve onboarding and offboarding touchpoints, collecting feedback and refining the journey
  • Support DE&I initiatives by tracking actions, assisting with communications, and gathering relevant data
  • Provide admin support to our Talent Development Manager (e.g. training invites, attendance tracking, feedback forms, learning records)
  • Help prepare materials, calendars, or surveys for employee feedback and engagement initiatives
  • Support with planning and delivery of employee events, celebrations, and engagement moments
  • Collaborate across People, Office, Internal Comms, ESG, and Talent Development teams to support broader cross-functional projects
  • Provide cover for the Office Manager during planned absences


Requirements/ Knowledge, Skills, and Experience:

You’re naturally structured, curious and eager to build a career in HR. You ask thoughtful questions, enjoy bringing order to complexity, and like figuring out how things work - and how they could work better. You’re confident working with systems and spreadsheets, have a sharp eye for detail, and thrive in environments where you can take ownership and make a real impact.


This role touches every part of the employee experience - from onboarding and benefits to data reporting and employee relations. You'll need to be comfortable working with sensitive situations, following clear processes, and supporting others with professionalism and care.


You might describe yourself as:

  • Tech-savvy and confident with data - you’re comfortable in spreadsheets and dashboards, and enjoy using digital tools to make things more efficient
  • Organised and methodical, with strong attention to detail - especially when it comes to admin, systems and tracking the small things that keep operations running smoothly
  • Curious and proactive - you notice when things could be improved, and you speak up with ideas
  • Discreet and people-aware - you handle sensitive information with integrity and know how to support people with empathy and professionalism
  • A fast learner who picks up systems easily, likes taking ownership of your work and enjoys taking initiative


What We Offer

  • A varied, generalist role where you’ll learn something new every day
  • Real ownership from day one, with space to develop and grow
  • Flexible hybrid working (with travel between offices Lower Eashing and London as needed)


We are united by a shared purpose and are committed to truly understanding each other. We know that everyone is unique and has their own story. We strive to have a diverse workforce that embraces and celebrates one another. We are united in building connections and curious to learn from each other so that we continue to grow together to build the workplace of tomorrow.


Why Work For Unily?


In addition to a generous base salary and discretionary company bonus, here are some things we think you will love:


Our awesome team culture. We are focused on achieving results as a team and having fun while we do it. You won’t find a friendlier or more dedicated bunch of people.


Our industry leading product. We are very proud of our ever-evolving product, naturally we use (and love) it internally and provide the tools and resources for you (and our clients) to become a Unily expert.


The flexibility that we offer. We don’t just mean working from home occasionally. We operate on a hybrid basis, and also recognize that life happens during the 9-5.30 and encourage a sustainable work/life balance.


Our bright and modern office spaces. When you need to be in the office we want it to be like being at home. We have a well-stocked kitchen and the option to bring your dog to work.


We offer a fantastic suite of benefits. Including 25 days holiday plus an extra paid day off to enjoy your birthday, Vitality life cover (for health, sight, hearing and dental), Aviva pension (via a salary sacrifice scheme), life assurance, income protection and so many more.


Our commitment to sustainability and giving back to the community. We know working for an organisation that takes its environmental & social impact seriously is important, and we are proud to offer 1 fully paid volunteering day per year, an employee matching charity donation scheme and options to lease an Electric Vehicle through our salary sacrifice scheme.


View Unily's UK & EEA Careers Privacy Notice here

View Unily's USA Careers Privacy Notice here

descriptionHtml:
Wise May are looking for a collaborative Compliance Officer with strong, current experience working in compliance within the Insurance industry to join a leading provider of legal indemnity Insurance. As a Compliance Officer you will be responsible for the co-ordination of policies, procedures and business standards to ensure compliance with the financial regulator, commercial agreements i.e. principal insurers’ delegated authority arrangements, legislation and business model/framework.


This is a full time, permanent role, with hybrid working (3 days a week in the office and 2 days remote) and the hours are 9am-5.30pm.


Compliance Officer duties and responsibilities:

  • Ownership of the delegated underwriting agreements and joint ownership with the Claims Manager of the delegated claims handling agreements, liaising as necessary with principal insurers in the negotiation and renewal of such agreements.
  • Monitor and advise the Business on all relevant legislative and compliance developments impacting on the Business.
  • Regularly review all regulatory and statutory materials relevant to the Business.
  • Update the Business with all appropriate regulation and legislation in timely manner.
  • Investigate compliance risks and issues and provide feedback as necessary.
  • Provide compliance advice, guidance and support, assist in seeking resolution of compliance issues including any new regulatory initiatives, and provide education and training to the Business as necessary.
  • Ensure that procedure manuals and policies are kept up to date and accurate, and they reflect the current regulatory and legislative requirements.
  • Respond to ad hoc queries working with business managers to assist in delivering regulatory compliance, monitoring compliance and general administration.
  • Create an audit framework which is suitable for the Business and undertake monitoring reviews ensuring that suitable solutions are implemented to address gaps or weaknesses identified.
  • Be the liaison point for external audits/reviews, monitoring agreed remedial actions following such reviews and reporting progress.
  • Assist with the assessment of adequacy or internal processes procedures and systems to ensure compliance with regulation and legislation and record keeping to better evidence compliance.
  • Provide compliance advice on the design and structure of products services and procedures.
  • Assist in the handling of all complaints ensuring all ‘lessons learnt’ are actioned; providing the regular and ad hoc reports.
  • Liaise with Insurers on all matters of compliance and individual authorities and facilitate the implementation of any changes required in accordance with business protocols.
  • Maintain close working relationships with the operational teams to ensure that suitable compliance support is provided in order to optimise Business activity.


Compliance Officer key skills and experience required:

  • Current Compliance / Audit experience in an insurance setting (MGA, Broker, Insurer, Lloyds)
  • Knowledge and/or experience in drafting service level agreements and delegated underwriting authorities is highly desirable.
  • Strong working knowledge of FCA guidelines, and relevant legislative frameworks.
  • Relevant qualification e.g. ACII or ICA or progress towards this is highly desirable.
  • Familiarity with compliance monitoring systems and methodology.
  • Excellent analytical, communication, and organisational skills.
  • Independent, confident and ability to liaise and communicate with staff and clients at all levels.
  • Committed to quality.
  • Competent user of Microsoft Office packages


Benefits

  • Discretionary bonus scheme
  • Salary sacrifice pension scheme
  • Private medical insurance
  • Life cover
  • Hybrid working opportunities

companyDescription:
Montagu Evans is an independent property consultancy, owned and run by a group of partners that are leaders in their respective fields and who are committed to leaving a legacy of quality work that benefits clients, the built environment and society as a whole. 

At our firm, the owners of the business do the work, meet the clients and have the freedom to assemble their own teams to suit each unique requirement.
 
This highly flexible and collaborative approach, tailoring the talent to the complexity of the work, has enabled us to develop a business that has spanned nearly a century. 

Our work encompasses planning and development, property management, specialist valuations and transactions. Together we create inspiring places to live, work, communicate and connect. We care about legacy, partnerships and people. Advising clients with complex planning and development challenges.
companySlogan: A property consultancy. A partnership. We care about people. About spaces to live work, communicate and connect.
descriptionHtml:
THE FIRM 

 

Montagu Evans is an independent, market leading property consultancy, headquartered in London with offices in Scotland and Manchester.  Our work encompasses planning and development, specialist valuations and transactions across the United Kingdom. We’re a group of people who are excellent individually and exceptional together. We care about people, not just bricks and mortar. We care about our legacy. We create spaces that people use to live, work, communicate and connect.

 

We’re a partnership and the people who run the business are empowered to create collaborative teams to exceed our clients' requests. This highly flexible approach, tailoring the talent to the complexity of the work, has enabled us to develop an exceptional business.

 

We encourage an honest and straight forward approach to our work and relationships. We embrace individuality, whilst relying on working together and never accepting the status quo. We give everyone the opportunity to be the best version of themselves. We trust and support each other. We recognise that our people are the heart of our success and only by identifying, supporting and promoting them will we be able to provide continued outstanding client service.

 

Montagu Evans is committed to maintaining an inclusive work environment. One that embraces everyone.

 

Read more here https://www.montagu-evans.co.uk/about/diversity/

 

ROLE OVERVIEW 

 

This is role sits within the Risk & Compliance team which plays a key role in the firm's governance, risk management and assurance arrangements. As a property consultancy and estate agent, the firm is regulated by the Royal Institution of Chartered Surveyors (RICS) for its surveying services, the Royal Town Planning Institute (RTPI) for its town planning services and supervised by HMRC for Anti-Money Laundering regulatory compliance purposes.

 

The Audit & Assurance Specialist plays a key role in providing independent and objective evaluations of the firm’s governance and risk management arrangements by conducting an agreed plan of internal audits across the year to drive improvement and provide assurance to stakeholders. The Audit & Assurance Specialist also leads the relationship with ISO external auditors and the firm’s preparations for the ISO recertifications.

 

Knowledge of Internal Auditing Standards and experience in their application is essential. A minimum of two years' internal auditing experience, including compliance audits, risk-based audits, and ISO certifications-related audits is also essential.

 

The role reports into and is overseen by the Head of Compliance.

 

 

 

MAIN DUTIES & RESPONSIBILITIES 

 

  • Develop an effective working relationship with the Head of Compliance. Effective and timely communication is essential. Lead regular progress update meetings with the Head of Compliance and arrange ad hoc meetings as and when required.
  • Lead the annual internal audit planning process. Prepare the draft annual internal audit plan, including its resource requirements, at least 3 months prior to its planned start. Present this to the Head of Compliance for review, and then to other senior stakeholders for discussion and approval.
  • Lead the delivery of the internal audit plan to ensure all planned audits are completed in a timely and efficient manner, avoiding unnecessary delays that may impact the business. Provide monthly progress updates to the Head of Compliance.
  • Ensure individual internal audits are planned and completed in accordance with the agreed annual internal audit plan. This includes drafting and agreeing the Terms of Reference/Plan for each audit with the Head of Compliance, timely stakeholder engagement, prompt audit delivery, drafting impactful draft internal audit reports, and presenting these to the Head of Compliance for review and discussion prior to issue.
  • Develop strong working relationships with internal and external stakeholders. Engage on a timely basis to obtain information and support required to complete planned audits.
  • Lead the relationship with the LRQA ISO auditors and other audit service providers. Oversee the ISO certification audits, including all pre-audit planning and stakeholder engagement prior to and during the onsite LRQA auditor visits to ensure the firm maintains its ISO certifications.
  • Maintain and keep under review a schedule of agreed actions to address audit findings. Conduct ongoing monitoring and periodic review of agreed actions with relevant Action Owners to ensure their timely implementation.
  • Identify and escalate concerns to the attention of the Head of Compliance.
  • Provide compliance-related advice and support to stakeholders across the business.
  • Contribute to the development and delivery of compliance training to new starters and others as required.
  • Keep up to date with internal and external regulatory developments that may impact the firm's regulatory compliance. Contribute to the identification of compliance risks or gaps and develop proposals to mitigate these risks.

 

 

KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS, EXPERIENCE & QUALIFICATION

 

  • In depth knowledge of IIA Standards and of the requirements of the ISO certification framework
  • A relevant internal audit qualification with a minimum of 2 years PQE
  • Proven experience in internal audit planning, execution, reporting and actions monitoring
  • Proven experience in conducting compliance audits, risk-based audits and ISO certification-related audits
  • A confident and personable manner, with excellent written and verbal communication skills
  • Proven ability to communicate the benefits of internal audits to a wide range of stakeholders and to drive change
  • Excellent workload management skills to meet strict deadlines
  • Determination and resilience to resolve challenging matters in a timely manner
  • Strong attention to detail.
  • Ability to work independently with minimal supervision.

 

Desirable:

  • Previous experience in the Commercial Real Estate sector

Global Law Firm

My client, a globally recognised law firm, is actively recruiting for an experienced Legal Risk & Compliance professional to join them on a permanent basis in their London office - working as their new Risk & Compliance Officer.

For more information on this position and to review a full job-description, get in touch as soon as possible!

descriptionHtml:
Company Description

Experian is a global data and technology company, powering opportunities for people and businesses around the world. We help to redefine lending practices, uncover and prevent fraud, simplify healthcare, create marketing solutions, and gain deeper insights into the automotive market, all using our unique combination of data, analytics and software. We also assist millions of people to achieve their financial goals and help them save time and money.

We operate across a range of markets, from financial services to healthcare, automotive, agribusiness, insurance, and many more industry segments.

We invest in people and new advanced technologies to unlock the power of data. As a FTSE 100 Index company listed on the London Stock Exchange (EXPN), we have a team of 22,500 people across 32 countries. Our corporate headquarters are in Dublin, Ireland. Learn more at experianplc.com.

Internal Grade D

Job Description

Experian Data Office are looking for a Project Manager for a 12 month fixed-term contract.

You will lead the delivery of a portfolio of projects within the Data Management function, ensuring agreement on strategic goals and the realisation of measurable benefits.

These projects are necessary for driving operational efficiency, you will manage costs, enhancing oversight of data use and spend, and support industry transformation. Through structured governance and proactive partner engagement, the role will coordinate delivery, manage risks, and embed sustainable outcomes across the organisation.

This is a Nottingham based hybrid position reporting to the Head of Data Management

Important Responsibilities:-

Programme Leadership

  • Define and maintain the programme structure, ensuring understanding of strategic goals
  • Translate team goals into a set of projects and workstreams with clear scope, deliverables and owners
  • Provide direction and oversight to workstreams, ensuring consistency in delivery.
  • Monitor progress across the programme, resolving issues and escalating risks.

Partner Engagement

  • Manage relationships with partners across the Data Office and also the wider business (legal, compliance, technology)
  • Facilitate regular communication to ensure understanding, manage expectations, and secure agreement for programme goals
  • Be a trusted advisor to senior leadership, providing insight into programme performance and strategic effects
  • Lead workshops, steering groups, and partner forums to guide collaboration.
  • Ensure accurate reporting of programme status, risks, and dependencies to senior partners
  • Support assurance activities, including internal audits and external reviews.

Benefits Realisation

  • Undertake analysis to support projects and the eventual benefits realisation
  • Define and track programme benefits, ensuring they are measurable, realistic, and aligned with team & our goals
  • Work with business owners to embed changes and ensure benefits are realised and sustained post-delivery
  • Conduct post-implementation reviews to assess outcomes and identify lessons learned

Continuous Improvement

  • Promote a culture of learning and improvement across the Data Office, demonstrating the value of tools such as EmPower or Lean Six Sigma
  • Identify improvements to both BAU & programme delivery methods, tools, and templates
  • Share best practices and lessons learned across teams to enhance future delivery and to help develop others within the team
  • Stay informed of industry trends and practices in programme and change management.

Qualifications

  • Degree or relevant experience in a similar role such as Business/Data Analysis, Process Consultant, Project Manager
  • Programme or project management certification (e.g., MSP, PRINCE2, PMP)
  • Familiarity with Lean, Six Sigma, or Agile methodologies (e.g. EmPower)

Additional Information

Benefits include:-

  • Flexible work environment, working hybrid or in the office if you prefer.
  • Great compensation package and discretionary bonus plan
  • Core benefits include pension, bupa healthcare, sharesave scheme and more
  • 25 days annual leave with 8 bank holidays and 3 volunteering days. You can purchase additional annual leave.
  • Opt-in options for dental insurance, healthcare cash plan

Experian is proud to be an Equal Opportunity and Affirmative Action employer. Innovation is an important part of Experian's DNA and practices, and our diverse workforce drives our success. Everyone can succeed at Experian and bring their whole self to work, irrespective of their gender, ethnicity, religion, colour, sexuality, physical ability or age. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know at the earliest opportunity.

Experian Careers - Creating a better tomorrow together

Find out what its like to work for Experian by clicking here

companyDescription:
Most businesses are terrible at communicating with their customers. They either don't communicate enough, or use channels their customers don't like. But when they get it right, it can transform their customer experience and their financial performance. So, it's our mission to make business communication brilliant.

Commify is Europe's leading supplier of Application-To-Person business messaging services. We help over 46,000 companies communicate brilliantly with their customers and staff; sending almost 5 billion messages a year through our portfolio of brands.

Our messaging solutions encompass everything from SMS to mobile payments, accessed through web applications, APIs or bespoke integrations. All of our products and platforms are supported by a passionate and innovative international team, with in-house development and support. 

Headquartered in Nottingham (UK), we sell through a portfolio of brands, dependent on geography and customer segment. These include Esendex,  TextAnywhere, Text Marketer, Skebby, Massenversand, SMSpubli, SMS Envoi, and Spryng and operate in the UK, Ireland, Italy, France, Spain, Germany, The Netherlands, Australia, New Zealand and The United States. 

Backed by ECI Partners, we continue to innovate and grow both existing and new markets to support more companies to transform their business communications.
companySlogan: Making business communication brilliant
descriptionHtml:
Join Commify, a global leader in communication technology, as our Group Financial Controller. In this critical role, you will lead the overall financial operations of the company, ensuring the integrity of financial reporting, compliance, and robust financial management across our diverse international portfolio. Your strategic vision will contribute significantly to our mission of delivering exceptional communication solutions to businesses around the world.

As the Group Financial Controller, you will oversee the preparation of consolidated financial statements, facilitate budgeting and forecasting processes, and maintain strong relationships with both internal and external stakeholders. This is a fantastic opportunity to influence the financial strategy of a rapidly growing company while mentoring a talented finance team.

Requirements

The ideal candidate for the Group Financial Controller position will have a solid background in finance and accounting, with the following key responsibilities:

Group Reporting & Consolidation:

  • Lead and manage the end-to-end consolidation process for both management accounts and statutory financial statements for all global entities
  • Ensure timely, accurate, and compliant financial reporting in accordance with relevant accounting standards (e.g., IFRS, GAAP)
  • Drive continuous improvement in consolidation processes, systems, and controls
  • Take full responsibility for the accurate accounting and financial management of all Head Office entities

Audit & Compliance:

  • Act as the primary liaison with external auditors, managing the annual group audit process and ensuring all audit requirements are met efficiently
  • Oversee internal control frameworks, ensuring robust financial controls are in place and adhered to across the group
  • Ensure compliance with all relevant financial regulations and statutory requirements globally

Tax & Transfer Pricing:

  • Lead and manage all aspects of group corporate tax compliance
  • Develop, implement, and maintain the group's transfer pricing policies and documentation, ensuring compliance with international tax regulations
  • Work closely with external tax advisors and internal stakeholders on tax planning and optimisation initiatives

Treasury and Covenants:

  • Own the quarterly covenants submission to the external lenders and ensure full compliance will all aspects of the Group's external debt Facilities Agreement
  • Manage banking relationships, cash pooling, and ensuring efficient treasury operations
  • Oversee and enhance the group-wide cash forecasting process, providing accurate and timely insights into liquidity and working capital management

Mergers & Acquisitions (M&A) Integration:

  • Play a role in the financial due diligence and integration of acquired businesses, ensuring seamless financial onboarding and reporting alignment
  • Lead the accounting for business combinations and purchase price allocation
  • Develop and implement financial integration plans post-acquisition

Systems & Process Management:

  • Own the management, optimisation, and integrity of the NetSuite ERP and Prophix consolidation tools across the group
  • Drive system enhancements and automation initiatives to improve efficiency and accuracy in financial processes
  • Ensure data quality and consistency within NetSuite and other financial systems

Team Leadership & Development:

  • Lead, mentor, and develop a high-performing finance team, fostering a culture of continuous improvement, accountability, and professional growth
  • Manage and oversee the day-to-day activities of the financial control team, ensuring efficient workflow and task allocation
  • Provide strong leadership and guidance to direct reports and the wider finance function

The successful candidate will possess:

  • Professional Qualification: Fully qualified accountant (ACA / ACCA or equivalent) with significant post-qualification experience and exceptional IFRS technical accounting knowledge
  • Senior Experience: Proven experience in a senior financial control role within a complex, multi-entity, global organisation
  • Team Leadership: Demonstrable experience in leading, managing, and developing a finance team
  • Audit & Tax: Strong experience in managing audits of international groups and comprehensive knowledge of corporate tax and transfer pricing principles
  • Consolidation Mastery: In-depth, hands-on experience leading and managing complex group consolidations (both management and statutory) - including FX elements
  • M&A Expertise: Demonstrable experience of the financial aspects of M&A transactions, including due diligence, integration, and purchase accounting
  • Global Business Exposure: Extensive experience working within a global business, understanding the complexities of international operations, currencies, and diverse regulatory environments
  • System Proficiency: Advanced ERP user (NetSuite preferred), with experience in system administration, configuration, and optimisation

Desirable:

  • Cash Management: Proven experience in cash forecasting, treasury operations, and banking administration
  • Private Equity Experience: Experience working in or with private equity-backed businesses, understanding the specific reporting demands, financial controls, and value creation drivers in such environments

Come be a part of our dynamic team at Commify, where your contributions will help reshape the future of business communication!

Benefits

  • Competitive Salary (£85 - 100,000)
  • Company bonus scheme
  • Comprehensive healthcare cash plan
  • A generous 27 days of annual leave in addition to Bank Holidays
  • 2 Wellbeing leave days and 2 days dedicated to giving back to your community
  • Enjoy your birthday off!
  • Employer pension contribution at 5%
  • Death in service benefit (4 times your salary)
  • Annual award recognition
  • Fun monthly and quarterly social events
  • Opportunities for training and professional development
  • Flexible hybrid working arrangement

companyDescription:
Rethinking banking to serve business ambition. 

Bank of London is the modern banking, payments and clearing partner for ambitious businesses, providing seamless, secure and innovative financial solutions.

We eliminate the complexity of traditional banking by leveraging cutting-edge technology to offer faster, more secure and more flexible financial solutions. Our approach removes legacy barriers, ensuring businesses can hold and move money effortlessly while staying focused on growth.

We empower businesses with smarter banking solutions that fuel ambition, remove friction and ensure financial safety and security.
companySlogan: Rethinking banking to serve business ambition.
descriptionHtml:
Bank of London offers a safer business banking model by holding all deposits at the Bank of England, eliminating the risk of 'bank runs.' It provides services such as Deposit, Embedded Banking, as well as Commercial Banking tailored to businesses. Powered by proprietary technology, the bank collaborates with SAP Fioneer to deliver innovative solutions. With a focus on exceptional service and core values of ownership, curiosity, the bank strives for excellence in all it does.

The role is a key leadership position, reporting into the COO and responsible for overseeing regulatory compliance with the COO org. You will represent a key component of our first line of defence and be responsible for the Banks’ Operational Resilience programme.

We are seeking a highly experienced Director of Compliance Operations to lead the first line of defence within our Operations function. This role will be responsible for compliance with regulatory obligations, including regulatory reporting, Single Client View (SCV) processes and reporting, RCSA methodology, and Operational Resilience. The successful candidate will play a key role in embedding a strong control environment, delivering robust risk and compliance oversight, and ensuring that critical business services within the payments function remain resilient.

You’ll bring strong communication skills, a collaborative mindset, and a proven ability to navigate complex problems. Success in this role will require resilience, an ability to prioritise, stakeholder management skills and an appetite to be challenged. You’ll thrive in a dynamic environment where responsiveness, relationship management, and operational excellence are key.

Responsibilities

  • Lead the first line of defence for compliance operations within the Operations function, ensuring adherence to all applicable regulations and internal policies.
  • Lead the Bank’s Operational Resilience capability, including identification of Important Business Services & Impact Tolerances, and ensuring end-to-end scenario testing, mapping, and lessons learned are embedded and maintained.
  • Be required to produce the Bank’s annual Operational Resilience Self-Assessment.
  • Be responsible for Operations’ regulatory reporting processes, including SCV reporting for deposit guarantee schemes, ensuring accuracy, timeliness, and audit readiness.
  • Be required to produce the Bank’s Annual Operational and Security Risk Report (REP018).
  • Oversee the process behind and be responsible for checking and submitting the Bank’s REP017, REP020, Complaints & Fraud Reports.
  • Own and maintain a robust RCSA process across Operations, ensuring appropriate risk identification, control testing, and remediation of control deficiencies.
  • Develop and manage control frameworks, operational risk indicators, and reporting dashboards to continuously monitor and improve the risk posture.
  • Within the Bank’s Operational Resilience framework, hold responsibility for the Bank’s BCP, ensuring business continuity plans and incident response procedures are aligned to resilience requirements and regulatory expectations.
  • Support with incident and issue management processes across Payments Operations, including root cause analysis, regulatory impact assessment, and timely remediation.
  • Collaborate with 2nd line Risk & Compliance and 3rd line Internal Audit to ensure alignment, gap closure, and independent assurance over controls and resilience planning.
  • Drive a culture of proactive risk and control management across the Bank through training, awareness, and leadership.
  • Support change and transformation initiatives, ensuring that risk, control, and resilience considerations are integrated from the outset.

Experience Required

  • Proven leadership experience in compliance, operational risk, or resilience roles within banking or financial services, preferably in Payments Operations.
  • Strong knowledge of UK regulatory frameworks including FSCS, PSR, PRA/FCA Operational Resilience policies, and other relevant bodies.
  • Strong understanding of SYSC 15A, SS1 21, SYSC 8 and SS2 21.
  • Hands-on experience implementing and maintaining RCSAs and operational risk control frameworks.
  • In-depth understanding of Operational Resilience principles, with a track record of delivering resilience frameworks in line with regulatory requirements.
  • Strong communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to influence across all levels.
  • Experience in leading complex operational functions through regulatory change, risk events, or resilience testing.
  • Strategic thinker with operational discipline and highly collaborative.
  • Strong leadership and people development skills.
  • Resilient and adaptable in a dynamic environment.

Location

  • London (5 days per week) or Belfast (4 days per week)
  • Travel to other locations may be required on occasion.

Diversity

The Bank of London is an equal opportunity employer committed to inclusion, diversity and belonging. All qualified applicants are welcome and will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, colour, age, religion or religious expression, sex, sexual orientation, gender or gender identity and expression, transgender, national origin, or military veteran status.

As the world of financial services becomes more automated, PAYALLY abides by the esteemed tradition of keeping the human touch to deliver exceptional service.  We make sure every client, regardless of size, needs and transaction volume, is looked after by a private Relationship Manager. 

We offer a fully integrated suite of financial services solutions that are tailor-made for each business with global ambitions. Our extensive network across the world allows us to provide frictionless cross-border payments in 39 currencies. Rest assured, your funds are secure with us because as an electronic money institution (EMI), we protect you through safeguarding.

We understand that not all international transfers are the same, and this is where our broad experience in the intricacies of operating an international business comes to your advantage. As your trusted partner, we listen, we recommend, we deliver. We offer you the flexibility to conduct foreign trading seamlessly whilst we take care of the myriad of details involved to move your funds.

Speak to us about joining our exclusive clientele.

descriptionHtml:
As the world of financial services becomes more automated, PayAlly abides by the esteemed tradition of keeping the human touch to deliver exceptional service. We make sure every client, regardless of size, needs and transaction volume, is looked after by a private Relationship Manager. PayAlly is a principle member of Visa and Mastercard. We offer a fully integrated suite of financial services solutions that are tailor-made for each business with global ambitions. Our extensive network across the world allows us to provide frictionless cross-border payments in 39 currencies. PayAlly provides international and local payments, multicurrency account, currency exchange, debit cards, card issuing and payments gateway.


This position combines oversight of compliance frameworks with responsibility for controls and monitoring. It reports to the Head of Compliance.


This role is a full-time (Monday-Friday) role based in London.

Hybrid working with a requirement to be in the office 2-3 times a week.


Main Responsibilities

  • Support the maintenance and ongoing monitoring of the Payally’s compliance strategy and framework, ensuring adherence to FCA rules, the PSD2 & EMRs, and Consumer Duty.
  • Oversee regulatory reporting obligations with the FCA and other relevant authorities.
  • Monitor and assess emerging regulatory risks, providing advice and updates to senior management and the Board.
  • Lead the compliance team and promote a culture of regulatory compliance and good conduct across the business.
  • Liaise with partner banks, card schemes (Visa/Mastercard), and financial institutions to address compliance requirements and integrate them into PayAlly’s risk and product infrastructure.
  • Develop, maintain, and review compliance policies, procedures, and monitoring programmes.
  • Conduct quality assurance reviews on key compliance activities (e.g., safeguarding, complaints handling, client disclosures, conduct of business).
  • Perform compliance risk assessments for new business initiatives and ongoing operations.
  • Support global compliance activities, particularly across the UK and Canada.
  • Chair or contribute to risk and compliance committees.
  • Work closely with internal audit, risk, and legal teams to ensure alignment with regulatory expectations.
  • Provide horizon scanning to identify and prepare for changes in the regulatory landscape.
  • Participate in ad-hoc compliance projects and tasks as assigned.


Required Experience & Qualifications

  • Proven experience in a senior compliance role within an electronic money institution
  • In-depth knowledge of FCA regulations, including SMF16 and SMF17 requirements
  • Experience in developing and implementing compliance frameworks
  • Strong leadership and communication skills, with the ability to influence at all levels
  • Professional qualifications in compliance or financial crime are advantageous
  • Comfortable in a fast-paced, changing environment

descriptionHtml:
Job Title: Quality & Compliance Officer

Location: Hertfordshire

Salary: £30,000 – £35,000 (DOE)


Recruiting on behalf of a leading organisation in the forensic and digital services sector, we are seeking a Quality & Compliance Officer to join a high-performing team. Reporting directly to the Chief Risk and Compliance Officer, this is a fantastic opportunity for someone looking to advance their career in quality, compliance, and risk management within a highly regulated and fast-evolving industry.


About the Organisation

Our client is a well-established and growing company that operates at the forefront of forensic analytics and digital services. They are committed to quality, innovation, and continuous improvement, and are proud to offer a collaborative, flexible working culture with excellent employee benefits.


The Role – What You’ll Do

As the Quality & Compliance Officer, you will support all quality and compliance functions and play a key role in maintaining and enhancing management systems across a number of standards and certifications, including ISO and regulatory codes. Responsibilities include:

  • Managing document control activities to ensure compliance with standards such as ISO 9001, 27001, 17020, 17025, and the Forensic Science Regulator’s Code of Practice.
  • Administering and maintaining the electronic Quality & Compliance Management System.
  • Supporting root cause analysis, investigations, and 5WHY methodology for non-conformance, complaints, and other quality issues.
  • Conducting internal audits and preparing relevant documentation and reports.
  • Supporting the production of KPIs, internal communications, and senior leadership reporting.
  • Engaging with external assessment and certification bodies.
  • Promoting continuous improvement initiatives including FMEA.
  • Building strong internal relationships across all departments to support quality and compliance goals.


Candidate Profile – What We’re Looking For

Essential:

  • Working knowledge of ISO standards (9001, 27001, 17025, etc.) and/or FSR CoP, or a strong willingness to undergo training.
  • Excellent organisational skills and ability to manage complex workloads.
  • Strong IT skills including MS Office (Excel, Outlook, PowerPoint, Word, Teams).
  • Proven ability to adapt to new systems and software tools.

Desirable:

  • Experience in a regulated or forensic environment.
  • Internal auditor qualification or audit experience.
  • Previous exposure to root cause analysis methods.
  • Background in process review and improvement.


What’s On Offer

  • £30,000 – £35,000 salary depending on experience
  • 25 days annual leave + 8 bank holidays
  • Birthday off
  • Company-wide Christmas shutdown
  • Enhanced parental and adoption leave
  • Flexible/agile working options
  • Private healthcare and regular eye tests
  • Mental health support & neurodiversity champions
  • Volunteering opportunities
  • Cycle to work scheme
  • Office dogs
  • Access to counselling support
  • Employee recognition and award programmes

descriptionHtml:
The post holder will support the delivery of services within the Long Term, Urgent, Cancer & Community Directorate. They will work closely with Directorate, Care Group and Department leadership teams to support capacity and demand planning and managing compliance against national standards and local driver/watch metrics.

This role is specifically focussed on operational delivery of Urgent and Emergency Care (UEC) services.

Good communication skills and being able to work with all levels of staff are crucial, with the ability to remain calm under pressure to meet deadlines.

Reporting to the Service Manager the post-holder will work to operationally manage the delivery of service key to delivery of UEC with some flexibility to support Acute, Medical and cancer services as required.

Duties will include monitoring pathways in identified specialties, operational managing delays which impact on achievement of ED and hospital flow, engaging with clinical staff to ensure job plans are effective and activity is managed in a timely manner, reporting performance data at relevant forums and supporting the senior team to produce relevant reports and updates.

At Harrogate and District NHS Foundation Trust, our values are Kindness, Integrity, Teamwork and Equality (KITE). If you are looking for a career in the NHS where you can make a real difference to patients' lives, this is the place for you.

  • Responsible for highlighting and addressing issues of concern, as appropriate
  • Support development of appropriate reporting and develop robust Validation processes
  • Provide training and communication in relation to capacity & demand modelling and performance against KPIs
  • Support the Triumvirate to delivery their Care Group's Driver Metrics as part of the HDFT Impact quality improvement methodology
  • Maintain Departmental governance processes in support of nursing and clinical leadership team(s)

Harrogate and District NHS Foundation Trust (HDFT) cares for the population in Harrogate and the local area, as well as across North Yorkshire, Leeds and parts of the North East.

At HDFT we provide outstanding care to both our patients and our staff and we value Kindness, Integrity, Teamwork and Equality. If you’re looking for an interesting and rewarding career in the NHS, where you can make a real difference to patients’ lives, HDFT could be the place for you!

We Offer Colleagues

  • A supportive culture so colleagues can bring their whole selves to work.
  • Staff Recognition - as well as regular appraisals, we recognise achievements with our Making a Difference & Team of the Month Awards, along with annual Colleague Recognition Awards.
  • Employee Support and wellbeing – we offer a wide range of staff benefits including an Employee Assistance Programme, counselling service and a fast-track Physiotherapy service.

Please Note

  • Due to high levels of interest, posts may close early if we reach a sufficient number of applications
  • Visa sponsorship is only available for certain roles. Please ensure the role you are interested in meets the criteria for a visa before submitting your application.
  • Please also be aware that the Trust does not offer relocation packages for Agenda for Change roles.

For further details / informal visits contact: Name: Tom Gill Job title: Service Manager Email address: tom.gill1@nhs.net Telephone number: 01423 885959

hybrid - 3 days a week in the office

descriptionHtml:
Job Title: Quality & Compliance Officer

Location: Hertfordshire

Salary: £30,000 – £35,000 (DOE)


Recruiting on behalf of a leading organisation in the forensic and digital services sector, we are seeking a Quality & Compliance Officer to join a high-performing team. Reporting directly to the Chief Risk and Compliance Officer, this is a fantastic opportunity for someone looking to advance their career in quality, compliance, and risk management within a highly regulated and fast-evolving industry.


About the Organisation

Our client is a well-established and growing company that operates at the forefront of forensic analytics and digital services. They are committed to quality, innovation, and continuous improvement, and are proud to offer a collaborative, flexible working culture with excellent employee benefits.


The Role – What You’ll Do

As the Quality & Compliance Officer, you will support all quality and compliance functions and play a key role in maintaining and enhancing management systems across a number of standards and certifications, including ISO and regulatory codes. Responsibilities include:

  • Managing document control activities to ensure compliance with standards such as ISO 9001, 27001, 17020, 17025, and the Forensic Science Regulator’s Code of Practice.
  • Administering and maintaining the electronic Quality & Compliance Management System.
  • Supporting root cause analysis, investigations, and 5WHY methodology for non-conformance, complaints, and other quality issues.
  • Conducting internal audits and preparing relevant documentation and reports.
  • Supporting the production of KPIs, internal communications, and senior leadership reporting.
  • Engaging with external assessment and certification bodies.
  • Promoting continuous improvement initiatives including FMEA.
  • Building strong internal relationships across all departments to support quality and compliance goals.


Candidate Profile – What We’re Looking For

Essential:

  • Working knowledge of ISO standards (9001, 27001, 17025, etc.) and/or FSR CoP, or a strong willingness to undergo training.
  • Excellent organisational skills and ability to manage complex workloads.
  • Strong IT skills including MS Office (Excel, Outlook, PowerPoint, Word, Teams).
  • Proven ability to adapt to new systems and software tools.

Desirable:

  • Experience in a regulated or forensic environment.
  • Internal auditor qualification or audit experience.
  • Previous exposure to root cause analysis methods.
  • Background in process review and improvement.


What’s On Offer

  • £30,000 – £35,000 salary depending on experience
  • 25 days annual leave + 8 bank holidays
  • Birthday off
  • Company-wide Christmas shutdown
  • Enhanced parental and adoption leave
  • Flexible/agile working options
  • Private healthcare and regular eye tests
  • Mental health support & neurodiversity champions
  • Volunteering opportunities
  • Cycle to work scheme
  • Office dogs
  • Access to counselling support
  • Employee recognition and award programmes

descriptionHtml:
About Us:

Statom Group Ltd. is a trusted leader in the civil engineering sector, specialising in Remediation, Groundwork, and Structures. We are committed to maintaining high standards of quality and integrity in all our projects. To support our operations, we are seeking a detail-oriented and proactive Finance and Document Controller to join our team.

Role Overview:

In this dual-function role, you will be responsible for managing both financial documentation and ensuring the accuracy and integrity of our financial records. You will play a pivotal part in facilitating smooth operations and compliance with financial practices while supporting project teams with document control. Your attention to detail and organisational skills will be crucial to the success of this position.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Maintain and manage financial records, including invoices, receipts, and expense reports
  • Assist in the preparation of financial reports and budgets for management review
  • Ensure proper filing and documentation of all financial transactions and supporting documents
  • Manage the document control process for project documentation, ensuring accuracy and compliance with regulations
  • Coordinate the review and approval of documents to ensure timely processing and distribution
  • Provide support to project teams in accessing and navigating document management systems
  • Collaborate with external auditors and finance teams as required

Requirements

Qualifications and Skills:

  • Experience in financial administration or document control within the construction or engineering sector
  • Strong understanding of financial processes, reporting, and compliance
  • Excellent organisational skills, with keen attention to detail
  • Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite (especially Excel) and experience with financial management software
  • Ability to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines effectively
  • Strong communication skills, both written and verbal
  • Ability to work collaboratively with both finance and project teams
  • A degree in finance, accounting, or related field is preferred

Benefits

Company Pension

Life Insurance

Private Medical

Perk Box

Holiday 22+8 BH

descriptionHtml:
We have a unique opportunity for an Underwriting Assistant to join our Insurance Operations team.

This role will sit within our EMEA COO function. The Service Delivery team play a vital role in supporting the various business lines within our Underwriting team based in our London, UK office whilst at the same time undertaking transformation activities with which to broaden, and better control the services provided. This exciting opportunity will allow the incumbent to play an integral part in strengthening the operational resilience of the services provided by the Operations function, whilst laying the foundations for a career in Insurance.

Location: This position will be based out of our London office. We strive for collaboration which is why we offer a work environment where our employees thrive and develop long lasting careers.

Our Business, Your Impact, Our Opportunity

What you'll be doing:

Data Collection and Entry: Gathering, validating, and inputting data will include the transposing of policy information from various documents to pricing platforms, policy administration systems or any other relevant systems.

  • Documentation Management: Preparing, organising, and maintaining policy documentation and underwriting files with a functional and working knowledge of ImageRight, Guidewire, Workflow, Document Management Systems, and pricing platforms.
  • Process Coordination: Coordinating with various departments and Outsourced providers to facilitate smooth workflow and timely processing as defined within Standard Operating Procedures. This will include:
    • The management of policies from submission management to booking.
    • Assistance in the preparation of renewals.
    • The management of cash balances.
    • Ensuring KPIs are met in relation to speed and quality.

  • Issue / Query resolution: Supporting effective service delivery, queries will be resolved within established timeframes. This includes:
    • Follow-up on missing documents/information from brokers to ensure completeness of the underwriting information.
    • Management of Outsourced providers queries and errors.
    • Compliance Adherence: Ensuring Standard Operating Procedures are followed, with related controls completed. Support will also be provided to internal/external audit teams and any auditing requirements.
    • Reporting: Input into the operational management dashboard in an accurate and timely manner.

Continuous Improvement: Working with other UAs in the Operations department to identify and document opportunities for continuous improvement and efficiency. This will include:

  • Performing functional testing of required IT & Process changes.
  • Participation in change / transformation activities as a Subject Matter Expert.
  • Training: Undertake all required training so as to understand the underwriting process, enabling effective support of the Underwriters. Also, support the training of new team members as they join the team.

What You'll Bring

  • Strong problem solving and decision-making abilities.
  • Excellent written and oral communication skills.
  • Exceptional interpersonal skills with the ability to work with a team oriented approach;
  • Ability to work effectively in a fast-paced environment.
  • Proactive mindset.
  • Eye for detail.
  • Logical thinker.
  • Strong time management.
  • Ability to organise efficiently.
  • Confidence to challenge.
  • Competent computer skills - must include Microsoft Excel, Microsoft Word and Microsoft Outlook as a minimum.

Working knowledge of Insurance Underwriting systems (to include PPL and Power BI) would be useful.

Desired Skills and Experience
Effective Communicator
Underwriting experience is desirable

No terminology in this advert is intended to discriminate on the grounds of gender, race, age, national origin, sexual orientation, and we confirm that we are happy to accept applications from persons of any age or experience for this role.
