descriptionHtml:
About The Role

Purpose

Reporting to Chief Risk Officer, the role will be responsible for assisting with the embedding of the Groupwide Risk Management Framework (RMF) and its components within Lync Wealth Management as appropriate. This would include elements of Risk, Compliance and Finance Crime Prevention.

Responsibilities

Risk & Compliance

  • Support the Chief Risk Officer (CRO) in discharging duties in line with legal, regulatory, and business requirements.
  • Lead and support the delivery, maintenance, and continuous improvement of the Risk Management Framework (RMF) and its supporting policies, procedures, and processes, ensuring it is embedded effectively across LWM.
  • Maintain and update the LWM Policy Matrix; ensure all associated Risk & Compliance policies (e.g., Incident Reporting Policy) remain current and demonstrate value.
  • Deliver and support in the preparation of clear and comprehensive risk and compliance reports, providing context, key risks/issues, and proposed mitigating actions where appropriate.
  • Lead in the development, oversight, and maintenance of LWM Risk Appetite Statements (RAS) and associated Key Indicators (KIs); lead on collation and reporting, engaging with business areas to agree actions where needed.
  • Oversee and embed the Bottom-Up Risk Map (BURM) Process across LWM, including tracking completion of mitigation actions; support LWM Top-Down Risk Map (TDRM) process.
  • Support and challenge the first line of defence in risk identification and management through ad hoc activities and structured RMF components (e.g., BURMs, incident management).
  • Undertake root cause and trend analysis, including deep dives as required by senior management, Committees, or the Board.
  • Oversee the resolution, root cause analysis, and reporting of incidents.
  • Support the timely delivery and ongoing refinement of Risk Management Information (MI) to internal and external stakeholders.
  • Provide guidance to the first line on regulatory obligations, general compliance, and evidencing the embeddedness of risk practices, including emerging risk identification.
  • Recommend and support enhancements to risk and compliance operating models, procedures, and practices to ensure alignment with business strategy and risk appetite.
  • Maintain comprehensive records using the Governance, Risk & Compliance (GRC) system (aCCelerate), demonstrating risk management development and tracking agreed actions.
  • Collaborate across the Chief Risk Office, LWM Management Team, and business units to support agile and scalable compliance and risk approaches. Support the oversight, due diligence, and risk management of third-party suppliers, including incident handling and business continuity assessments.
  • Support the review, management, and renewal of LWM insurance policies.
  • Support LWM in the execution of the networks compliance programs and assurance activity.
  • Support the LWM Data Privacy Point of Contact and Data Protection Steering Group in managing data subject rights requests, monitoring UK GDPR compliance, and maintaining a strong data protection culture.
  • Support implementation and oversight of data protection policies, guidance, and legal obligations across the LWM.
  • Contribute to the prevention and proportionate management of data protection risks.

Other Responsibilities

  • Support the coordination, administration, and attendance of the LWM Risk Management Committee meetings and other delegated Committees.
  • Engage with the wider business pragmatically to offer risk-based solutions to business problems, while influencing risk-aware culture.
  • Uphold the principles of the FCA’s Consumer Duty, promoting fair and beneficial client outcomes.
  • Provide a strong voice on matters of ethics, integrity, and corporate values.
  • Develop and maintain effective stakeholder relationships across all business levels, including LWM Management and 7IM Executive Committee level.

About You

Knowledge

  • Minimum 10 years of industry experience, with at least 5 years in risk management.
  • Deep understanding of RMF components: RCSA, Incident Management, Risk Appetite, and Key Indicators.
  • Strong knowledge of FCA rules relevant to asset/wealth/fund management.
  • Understanding of compliance, regulatory frameworks, and best practice in a regulated environment.
  • Knowledge of EU and UK GDPR and data protection requirements.
  • Experience in asset management with retail exposure preferable.
  • Broad commercial understanding and ability to contextualise risk across the business.

Qualifications

  • Educated to degree level (preferred).
  • Professional qualifications in Risk and/or Compliance (desirable).

Skills / Other Relevant Information

  • Demonstrated ability to communicate complex regulatory/risk concepts clearly and succinctly to both technical and non-technical audiences, including Executive Committee members.
  • Excellent report-writing and verbal communication skills.
  • Analytical mindset with the ability to understand and distil complex data and processes.
  • Strong planning, organisation, and prioritisation abilities.
  • Comfortable operating within a dynamic, fast-paced scale-up environment with agility and pragmatism.
  • IT literate, with strong skills in Microsoft Word, Excel, and PowerPoint.
  • Demonstrated ability to work independently and collaboratively; builds strong cross-functional working relationships.
  • Committed to high ethical standards and aligned with organisational goals and values.
  • Enthusiastic about learning and expanding knowledge of 7IM’s wider business operations and strategic objectives.

About Us

Not Specified

descriptionHtml:
🚨 Urgent Hire – Compliance Officer / MLRO | Digital Assets 🚨


We’re supporting a fast-growing digital assets firm that is seeking to appoint a Compliance Officer / MLRO on a 3–4 day per week basis (with scope to increase over time). This is a critical leadership hire and an exceptional opportunity to help shape the compliance and risk culture of an ambitious, innovative business.



Key Responsibilities


  • Act as Compliance Officer and MLRO, holding responsibility for all compliance and financial crime frameworks.
  • Serve as the firm’s primary contact with regulators and industry bodies.
  • Design, implement, and oversee AML/CTF policies, systems, and controls tailored to digital assets.
  • Provide strategic compliance advice to the Board and senior management on regulatory developments.
  • Lead on risk management, ensuring operational and regulatory risks are identified, assessed, and mitigated.
  • Manage regulatory reporting, governance oversight, and compliance monitoring.
  • Drive a culture of compliance across the firm, training staff and embedding best practice.


Requirements


  • Previous experience as Compliance Officer and/or MLRO within a regulated Crypto or Payments firm, or alternatively holding SMF16/SMF17 approval.
  • Strong track record of managing compliance and financial crime obligations in a regulated environment.
  • Ability to engage confidently with regulators, demonstrating credibility and authority.
  • Deep understanding of UK AML/CTF regulation and the evolving regulatory landscape for digital assets.
  • Pragmatic, hands-on approach – able to operate strategically while rolling up sleeves when needed.



The Offer


  • Part-time engagement (3–4 days a week), with scope to expand as the firm grows.
  • Opportunity to join a firm at a critical inflection point, directly influencing its regulatory and operational future.
  • Interviews are being conducted this week – immediate availability is highly valued.


If you are an experienced compliance professional ready to take on a leadership role in the rapidly growing digital assets sector, please get in touch with me directly today.

descriptionHtml:
A boutique investment company is seeking a highly capable Number Two in Finance to support the Group Finance Director and play a pivotal role in leading the department. This is a hands-on position requiring a technically strong finance professional with proven experience across multi-entity structures, regulatory compliance, and process improvement.

This opportunity offers exposure to a broad and dynamic portfolio of businesses, where you will be instrumental in shaping financial operations, driving efficiency, and ensuring robust governance.


Key Responsibilities


  • Implement and manage a comprehensive Financial Management Accounting (FMA) system for the group and its subsidiaries.
  • Consolidate and oversee financial management across all group entities, ensuring integration and consistency.
  • Develop and deliver budgets, forecasts, and variance analysis, providing actionable insights to leadership.
  • Lead cash flow management, working capital optimization, and financial planning processes.
  • Produce consolidated financial statements, KPIs, and detailed management reports.
  • Oversee internal and external audits across all entities.
  • Act as the primary contact for external stakeholders, including banks, auditors, and tax authorities.
  • Mentor and provide leadership to finance teams, fostering accountability and compliance.
  • Ensure compliance with all financial regulations across the group, including finance and insurance divisions.
  • Manage client account operations, ensuring proper segregation, reconciliations, and audit readiness.
  • Leverage technology to enhance reporting, compliance tracking, and overall efficiency

.

Qualifications and Experience


  • Professional accounting qualification (ACA, ACCA, CIMA or equivalent).
  • 7–10 years’ senior financial management experience, ideally within legal, finance, or insurance sectors.
  • Strong track record in implementing and managing Financial Management Accounting systems.
  • Proven ability to oversee multi-entity or group structures.
  • Advanced skills in financial management software and Excel.
  • Deep understanding of regulatory requirements within finance and insurance.
  • Strategic thinker with a hands-on, operational approach.

descriptionHtml:
Location Regionally based role with frequent travel across the Home Counties & South West

Hours 37.5 hours per week working Monday - Friday (plus on-call on a rota basis)

Salary £45,000 - £55,000 per annum + car allowance & enhanced benefits package

Basis 6 month fixed term contract

About us

We are Pegasus Homes Ltd, a vibrant, dynamic business who are proud to design, build, and create high-quality homes with a focus on independent later living for the over 60's. Our developments are beautifully designed offering stunning community living.

We have a great opportunity for a Building & Communities Manager to join our property management team.

About the role

Our Buildings & Communities Managers work regionally managing onsite teams and managing the day to day operations for a portfolio of stunning later living developments. This role is critical in supporting the Pegasus Homes in creating positive, customer experiences and ensuring high quality, well maintained, compliant and well managed buildings.

You will manage, lead and support your area team to embrace every touch point potential customers, our owners and tenants and the public at large may have with our business as well as balance the requirement to monitor and justify spending, ensuring quality and value for money are at the forefront of everything we do.

Ideally you'll be based within a reasonable commuting distance of the Home Counties, with a willingness to visit developments in Devon & Cornwall on a monthly or bi-monthly basis.

Key responsibilities

  • Management of your area team to ensure consistency of approach from recruitment of new employees through to undertaking annual appraisals, putting in place development plans/opportunities, performance management, and ensuring collaboration and sharing of knowledge between team members
  • Ensuring building compliance for each development within the area, ensuring all documentation is kept up to date against both regulatory and statutory requirements and effective reporting is undertaken and delivered
  • Working alongside finance, input into budgets and operational expenditure for area developments, producing quarterly reports, inputting into the year end accounts and communicating to home owners as required
  • Lead on the mobilisation process to ensure our new sites are ready on time, home user guides have been developed and communicated and team members have been effectively trained so that the operation runs smoothly once our sites are live
  • Work with our procurement team on procuring services, agreeing the scope of works and reporting on quality of service provided by contractors
  • Work collaboratively with the sales team to ensure customers, homeowners and tenants are satisfied with their Pegasus Homes journey, their homes and their communities and have a positive ongoing relationship with us
  • Handle customer complaints/queries in a timely manner and in line with our customer complaints policy and support the team with other complaints and issues
  • Contribute to generating new ideas and commercially viable concepts to improve our customer journey

Please note You will be contractually based from home however this role will require frequent travel within a wide geographical region which includes the home counties and South West so a willingness to travel and your own vehicle / full driving licence is essential.

About you
You'll be an experienced multi-site residential property manager with a strong understanding of health & safety regulations and compliance. You'll have experience in interpreting and preparing reports and plans including financial reports, service charge accounts, budgeting, works scheduling, workload allocation and performance planning.

You'll be passionate about people both in managing and leading your team, and engaging with our residents.

In addition we are looking for the following skills & experience{{:}}

  • Experience in conducting site inspections, ensuring Health & Safety regulations are adhered to and having a good working knowledge in building maintenance systems
  • Managing customer complaints and managing difficult conversations
  • Extensive leadership experience, leading a team across multiple sites
  • Experienced in the management and delivery of comprehensive and transparent service charges
  • Confident and commercially aware with excellent communication skills
  • Property related qualification such as IRPM
  • Full UK driving licence and own vehicle is essential

Our benefits & rewards

Along with a competitive salary we offer fantastic benefits which look after your Financial, Mental and Physical Wellbeing as we believe it is important that we are able to support our employees across all three of these elements in order to ensure an effective work life balance.

By joining us you'll enjoy a benefits package that includes{{:}}

  • Enhanced annual leave & holiday buying scheme
  • Contributory pension scheme with additional employer contribution,
  • Life Assurance
  • Two paid volunteering days per annum
  • Employee Assistance Programme
  • Health Cash Plan & Virtual GP
  • Discounted Gym Membership
  • Company Discounts Portal
  • Refer a friend scheme and access to internal opportunities

If you have the skills and experience we are looking for we'd love to hear from you!

As an equal opportunities employer, we are committed to the equal treatment of all current and prospective employees and we do not condone discrimination on the basis of age, disability, sex, sexual orientation, pregnancy and maternity, race or ethnicity, religion or belief, gender identity, or marriage and civil partnership.

We aspire to have a diverse and inclusive workplace and strongly encourage suitably qualified applicants from a wide range of backgrounds to apply and join us. 

As an equal opportunities employer, Pegasus Homes welcomes applications from all sectors of the community. In line with UK Immigration and Employment Law, only applicants eligible to live and work in the UK need apply.

Requirements

descriptionHtml:
Our client, one of the UK’s largest asset managers with ambitions to double AUM, are looking to appoint an experienced Fund Compliance Specialist in a broad and rewarding role, covering both assurance and advisory activities.


Reporting directly to the Head of Risk & Compliance, the role will also support the firm’s wider strategic ambitions, including through engagement in the development of exciting new propositions. This is an opportunity to join a dynamic and ambitious firm in a varied Compliance role with exposure to senior stakeholders, both internally and externally.


You will:

  • Advise business areas on recommended control improvement actions.
  • Provide investment risk and compliance consultancy to key stakeholders
  • Lead on second line engagement change initiatives.
  • Support the Head of Risk and Compliance in the development and delivery of the Compliance Plan.
  • Review daily risk measurement checks (e.g. Derivative exposures; fund compliance with VaR limits, VaR back-testing overshoots and Liquidity thresholds)
  • Act as the risk and compliance contact for error and breach identification, resolution, trend analysis and escalation.
  • Liaise with the Depositary to complete regulatory and best practice obligations.


The ideal candidate will have demonstrable Compliance experience in the investment management sector, particularly in an ACD business, with deep knowledge of UCITS & COLL; Strong understanding and experience of 2LoD compliance monitoring techniques and regulatory requirements, including investment principles, investment types and portfolio liquidity; Excellent communication skills, with an ability to influence, facilitate and negotiate.

descriptionHtml:
We are seeking an experienced and detail-oriented Deputy Head of Compliance to join a UK-based banking institution. This role plays a key part in ensuring regulatory compliance and effective risk management across the organisation. You will support the ongoing development of compliance frameworks, provide guidance on regulatory requirements, and help maintain a culture of good conduct and data protection.


Key Responsibilities:

  • Maintain a strong understanding of relevant legislation, PRA and FCA requirements, and applicable industry guidance within a banking context.
  • Identify and assess regulatory risks, ensuring appropriate mitigation controls are in place and effectively maintained.
  • Keep the Compliance Risk and Control Self-Assessments (RCSAs) current and comprehensive.
  • Monitor and track changes in the regulatory landscape, assessing their impact on the business.
  • Support the planning, execution, and documentation of the compliance monitoring programme.
  • Implement and oversee conduct risk controls, identifying and addressing emerging issues, and ensuring regular reporting to governance bodies.
  • Provide training and expert advice to business teams on conduct risk and regulatory expectations.
  • Maintain and enhance the Conduct Risk Framework, aligning it with evolving regulatory and market conditions.
  • Regularly review and update compliance-related policies and procedures to ensure they remain effective and relevant.
  • Advise business areas on Consumer Duty and Treating Customers Fairly (TCF) obligations, supporting a compliant and customer-centric culture.
  • Build strong, collaborative relationships with key stakeholders across the organisation.
  • Ensure regulatory responsibilities are clearly communicated and understood by all staff through targeted training and awareness initiatives.
  • Produce monthly MI reports and updates related to compliance and conduct risk.
  • Serve as a key contact for data protection matters, supporting the Data Protection Officer in maintaining legal compliance.
  • Monitor adherence to UK GDPR and related legislation, supporting the implementation of data protection policies.
  • Investigate data breaches, ensuring timely and accurate reporting in accordance with internal procedures.
  • Deliver periodic data protection training across the business.


Ideal Candidate:

  • In-depth knowledge of FCA and PRA regulations, conduct risk, and UK data protection laws.
  • Strong understanding of compliance frameworks within the financial services sector.
  • Experience in delivering regulatory and conduct risk training.
  • Excellent communication skills and the ability to build effective cross-functional relationships.
  • A proactive, analytical approach to problem-solving and risk management.

companyDescription:
Our purpose is simple: To provide enhanced financial well-being for those who depend on us. We do that by seeking to deliver tailored, solutions-oriented private markets exposure and industry-leading client service. We’re an organization built on over 33 years of client-centricity, candor and authenticity and powered by intellectual rigor and data-driven insight.

Our commitment to serving those who depend on us has remained steadfast, while our focus on growth and transformation has only strengthened. Today, we serve 2,330+ institutional and private wealth investors around the world, helping them tap into the opportunities afforded by this maturing asset class.

*As of March 31, 2025

descriptionHtml:
Hamilton Lane is looking to expand our team to satisfy the needs of our growing client base. Hamilton Lane is built on collaboration, teamwork and integrity. Our employees pursue excellence and always strive to do the right thing. We invest in our employees, clients and partner relationships, as well as, in the technology and resources necessary to remain competitive, working in a competitive environment that inspires innovation.

What We Do

As a recognized leader in providing Private Markets Investment and Technology Solutions to clients across the globe, we manage approximately $134.9 billion in discretionary assets and have oversight of an additional $821.2 billion in non-discretionary assets as of December 31, 2024.

The Opportunity

As a Compliance Analyst, you will report to the Head of EMEA Compliance and support all rounded compliance queries for the EMEA region. You will also assist the EMEA team on risk based compliance reviews, development of compliance programs, guidelines, and procedures, and be responsible for producing regular compliance reporting.

This position offers exposure to senior professionals and stakeholders across Hamilton Lane and provides an excellent opportunity for professional development in a collaborative and fast-paced environment.

Your Responsibilities Will Be To

  • Review and approve marketing material for use in the EMEA region
  • Respond to employee compliance queries, including personal account dealings, gifts, and entertainment queries
  • Research and track regulatory updates and drafting briefing notes
  • Assist with the design, implementation, and execution of the Compliance Monitoring Programme based on the Compliance Risk Assessment
  • Coordinate with internal groups in the collection of audit requests
  • Assist with drafting and maintain compliance standard policies and procedural documents
  • Support senior compliance team colleagues with various ad hoc regulatory changes and other projects as required

Your Background Will Include

  • Undergraduate degree, or equivalent training and experience. LLB highly desirable
  • 1-2 years of experience in a compliance role, ideally, within asset management or private markets, but open to other financial services
  • An understanding of the financial services industry and UK and European regulations, ideally with a focus on private equity
  • Proven communication and analytical skills
  • Proficient in Microsoft Office applications
  • Must be proactive, self-starter and able to interact with people who have varied personalities and work styles
  • Additional language skills desirable: German and/or Italian

Benefits

At Hamilton Lane, our benefits philosophy is simple: to provide our employees with a competitive suite of benefits and services to help navigate through the complexities and challenges of working, living, raising a family, and eventually retiring.

To Do This, Hamilton Lane Offers The Following Benefits

Enhancing Your Physical and Emotional Health

Employees have access to healthcare coverage, mental health resources, health & fitness reimbursement program, and Wellness Rewards Program.

Developing Your Career

Tuition and certification reimbursement programs are available, along with continual education and development trainings for you to grow with Hamilton Lane.

Supporting Your Family & Community

For our communities, Hamilton Lane provides paid time off to volunteer and compensates for referring qualified candidates that join our team.

For growing family, we offer an adoption reimbursement program, paid time off for new parents and newlyweds, and provide travel support for nursing parents.

Safeguarding Your Financial Wellbeing

Hamilton Lane contributes to retirement programs and offers an employee stock purchasing plan.

Hamilton Lane is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will be considered for employment without regard to their race, religion, ancestry, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, age, disability, marital status, domestic partner status, or medical condition. As a registered investment adviser, employees of Hamilton Lane may be subject to certain limitations on political contribution and personal investment activities.

Hamilton Lane is not accepting unsolicited assistance from search firms for this employment opportunity. Please, no phone calls or emails. All resumes submitted by search firms to any employee at Hamilton Lane via-email, the Internet or in any form and/or method without a valid written search agreement in place for this position will be deemed the sole property of Hamilton Lane. No fee shall be paid in the event the candidate is hired by Hamilton Lane as a result of the referral or through other means.

companyDescription:
We are TradingView, the world's most popular charting platform and the industry's forefront for financial visualization solutions. 100M+ traders worldwide use our platform as a go-to destination to chart, chat, and trade financial markets. 

Our product portfolio includes best-in-class charts, versatile commercial libraries, a social network, and many more tools for retail and business audiences. With 200M+ monthly visits, we are the #1 all-things investing website, and our solutions are used by fintech leaders such as Revolut, Binance, and CME Group.

We wouldn't be where we are without our tight-knit multinational team from 40+ countries, distributed globally, including across core offices in London, Malaga, and Tbilisi. To get the best, we offer relocation packages with competitive compensation and ensure a smooth onboarding through mentorship and clear career paths.

Reach out to us, and let’s grow together.
companySlogan: The #1 charting platform and social network for traders and investors worldwide
descriptionHtml:
About us!

TradingView was founded with a mission to empower people to achieve success on their own terms. We're building a space where anyone can maximize their chances in the financial markets — with the best tools and data at their fingertips. Our product portfolio includes the best-in-class market analysis platform, versatile charting solutions, a social network, and many more tools for retail and business audiences. TradingView is trusted by fintech leaders such as Revolut, Binance, and CME Group.

We're now the world's largest financial analysis platform — with 100 million users in over 180 countries (even Antarctica!). Every day, people from all over the world use TradingView for analysis, collaboration, and informed trading decisions. We foster a community-driven approach to financial education, welcoming everyone from seasoned traders to beginners.

And to keep building great things, we need great people. To stay on top of our game, we’re looking for passionate individuals who care deeply about product quality and want to shape tools used by millions. If you’re prepared to do your best work, collaborate with cutting-edge technologies, and make an impact every day, look no further 🚀

Your mission: Make an impact 🎯

We are seeking a highly motivated and detail-oriented Accounts Receivable (AR) and Process Automation Specialist to join our finance team. This role will focus on streamlining and automating the accounts receivable processes while ensuring accuracy, efficiency, and timely cash flow. The ideal candidate will have a strong understanding of AR operations and experience with process automation tools to drive improvements.

How you’ll drive success 🏆

  • Accounts Receivable Management:
    • Oversee and manage the company’s AR functions, including invoicing, payment processing, and collections.
    • Monitor outstanding accounts and follow up with customers on overdue invoices, ensuring timely payments.
    • Reconcile customer accounts, resolve discrepancies, and work closely with customers to resolve billing issues.
    • Prepare and maintain accurate AR aging reports and provide insights into collections performance.
  • Process Automation:
    • Identify and implement opportunities for automation in the accounts receivable process to increase operational efficiency.
    • Work with finance and IT teams to design, test, and implement automated solutions such as invoicing, payment reminders, and collections workflows.
    • Use RPA (Robotic Process Automation), AI, and other automation tools to optimize repetitive tasks, such as data entry and report generation.
    • Develop and maintain dashboards and reports for real-time visibility into AR performance and KPIs.
  • Reporting & Analytics:
    • Provide regular reports on AR aging, cash flow projections, and collections activity to senior management.
    • Analyze trends in customer payments, identify patterns of delayed payments, and recommend actions for improvement.
    • Assist with month-end and year-end closing processes related to AR, including balance sheet reconciliations and reporting.
  • Collaboration:
    • Work closely with sales, customer service, and other departments to ensure accurate invoicing and effective communication with customers.
    • Collaborate with the IT department to ensure seamless integration of automation solutions into existing financial systems.
  • Continuous Improvement:
    • Stay up-to-date on industry best practices and emerging trends in AR and process automation.
    • Provide recommendations for process improvements and lead projects aimed at enhancing the efficiency of the AR department.
    • Drive the adoption of best practices for data integrity, compliance, and financial reporting.
  • Compliance & Risk Management:
    • Ensure compliance with internal controls, accounting policies, and external regulations related to AR processes.
    • Identify and address potential credit risk or fraud issues through proactive monitoring and analysis.

What makes you the perfect fit 🤝

  • Education & Experience:
    • Bachelor's degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field.
    • Minimum of 2-5 years of experience in accounts receivable, finance operations, or a similar role.
    • Proven experience with process automation in AR (e.g., RPA tools, ERP systems, AI-based solutions).
    • Familiarity with accounting software (e.g., NetSuite) and payment processing platforms.
  • Skills:
    • Strong understanding of accounts receivable processes and financial reporting.
    • Experience with process automation tools (e.g., UiPath, Automation Anywhere, Power Automate) preferred.
    • Advanced Excel skills (pivot tables, VLOOKUP, data analysis).
    • Analytical and problem-solving abilities.
    • Attention to detail and ability to handle complex financial data.
    • Strong communication skills and ability to collaborate with cross-functional teams.
  • Personal Traits:
    • Ability to work independently and manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.
    • Highly organized, with a focus on continuous improvement and driving efficiency.
    • Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to work effectively with i

It’ll also be an advantage if:

  • You have experience or knowledge of finance and trading.
  • You know about TradingView 😁. We love users who become teammates!

Additional Information

This is a hybrid role based in our Central London office, located in The Shard or in our Malaga office too.

About Our Perks And Benefits

  • Permanent Contract
  • Flexible Working Hours – Start your day when it suits you best within our wide range of entry hours.
  • Hybrid Work Policy – Enjoy the flexibility of combining remote and office work.
  • Relocation Package – Comprehensive support for a smooth transition for you and your family, including assistance with visa, transportation, and accommodation for our offices in Málaga and Tbilisi.
  • Private Health Insurance
  • Performance Bonus
  • Work alongside experienced professionals and mentors offering ongoing training and growth opportunities.
  • Premium TradingView Subscription
  • Annual Team Events – We love celebrating together!
  • A comfortable, well-equipped workspace with exclusive perks like a gym and much more!

TradingView is an equal opportunity employer. We embrace diversity and are dedicated to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace. Our success is driven by 500+ professionals from 40+ nationalities who speak nearly 20 languages and are spread across the globe. Our head offices are in London, New York, Málaga, and Tbilisi.

📩 Ready to make a real impact on the financial world? - Apply now and become part of a team that’s revolutionizing the way millions of people interact with the markets.

descriptionHtml:
Title of position:

Credit Risk Analyst - Corporate

Corporate Title:

Intern

Leadership Title (if applicable):

N/A

Department/Division:

Credit Portfolio and Resource Management UK


Overall purpose of the role:

To provide support for Portfolio Manager in first level credit risk analysis and portfolio management.

To develop your analytical skills and product knowledge.


Main responsibilities and duties of the role:

As an intern reporting to a senior analyst, you will be involved in supporting various aspects of credit risk management for a diverse portfolio of counterparties trading with us.

Your responsibilities will include:

  • Assisting in the initial and ongoing assessment of the creditworthiness of counterparties through financial analysis & internal credit scoring.
  • Providing support in making recommendations on the size and tenor of credit limits for approval.
  • Assisting in performing annual credit reviews for counterparties in the assigned portfolio, esp. in the commodities sector.
  • Building and maintaining strong working relationships with internal stakeholders in the business, coverage, legal, middle office, and risk.
  • Assisting in regular portfolio analysis, monitoring, and reporting.



Responsible for producing:

  • Draft credit analyses.
  • Portfolio reports.
  • Portfolio data.

Responsible for reviewing:

  • London portfolio reports - Corporate, Real Assets, and Asset & Structured Finance


Legal and Regulatory responsibilities

To comply with all applicable legal, regulatory and internal Compliance requirements including but not limited to

  • FCA Individual Conduct Rules
  • You must act with integrity
  • You must act with due skill, care and diligence
  • You must be open and cooperative with the FCA, the PRA and other regulators
  • You must pay due regard to the interests of customers and treat them fairly
  • You must observe proper standards of market conduct
  • FCA Senior Managers Rules
  • Where appropriate, PRA Senior Managers Rules
  • Market standards, codes and conduct requirements
  • Natixis compliance policies and procedures
  • Financial crime prevention requirements including reporting obligations to the Money Laundering Reporting Officer



JOB REQUIREMENTS - KEY SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE


Key skills, experience and personal attributes required (include problem solving aspects):


  • Degree qualified or currently pursuing a bachelor's degree in a related field.
  • Strong analytical and quantitative skills.
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Excel/Microsoft Office.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
  • Ability to work effectively in a team environment.
  • Familiarity with accounting and/or financial statements is advantageous.
  • Proficiency in English.


Specialist /technical or professional qualifications, including any registrations (FCA or other) required: N/A


Working conditions (If not standard working hours please state shift system used):

Contractual plus flexibility required in order to meet operational requirements and business needs

companyDescription:
Welcome to Neko London.

An independent agency with a big heart; Neko London works with the UK’s best creative agencies and brands; and the people that make them tick.

We have a simple mission; to help clients build great teams and candidates find companies they’ll love. This ensures our efforts are poured into reading between the lines to understand you and your needs. 

Neko’s expertise lie in representing people from Creative & Design, Account Management, and Creative Services through to Studio & Production. We have experience of working with a mix of clients; from Top 10 network agencies to boutique design and packaging specialists, global brands and everything in between.

The long-lasting relationships we have nurtured are imperative to Neko’s success, and something we take very seriously. We pride ourselves on honest communication and going above and beyond for your company or that all-important next step in your career. Neko will represent you with the highest level of integrity right from the start.
descriptionHtml:
Senior Account Director - Sustainability Reporting and Communications

London / UK remote

Salary £55,000 – 75,000 p/a


We are working with a leading agency with expertise in sustainability strategy and reporting as well as a creative communications offering. They provide a range of services around sustainability strategy, reporting (both voluntarily and for compliance), through to communications, PR and content for a diverse client roster. The agency is well established and has built a reputation based around its deep knowledge and experience in corporate Sustainability Programmes and reporting compliance. It's known for its consultative approach, well-crafted creative output and the emphasis it places on truly understanding the clients that they work with and communicating their goals with regards to Sustainability and Corporate Social Responsibility. This allows them to produce exceptional reports and communications work that is executed to the highest standard and has meant that they have retained clients as well as winning new ones year on year.


The agency in question are looking for a talented Sustainability Reporting Consultant at a Senior Account Director level, who also has some broader Communications experience to work with global clients including some of the world’s leading brands and well know organisations. You’ll work with clients to understand their sustainability strategy, programme materiality and the metrics and frameworks around these and produce high calibre reporting that is compliant and tells the story of their journey. You’ll need to be experienced at working on large scale reports and with frameworks like GRI, IFRS S1 and S2, ESRS under CSRD, UNGPRF, and TCFD/SASB, and any experience of integrated reporting would be an added bonus. You’ll also need to have exposure to communications planning around the release of reporting targeted at different groups of stakeholders.


The role requires someone with extremely strong project oversight skills as you work with multiple stakeholders at blue chip clients as well as internal analysts, copy & creative teams on the content of the report, required disclosures alongside the narrative and creative look and feel of the reporting. You’ll need to have very strong consultation / advisory skills and be a brilliant client leader who enjoys networking, and selling ideas and services into clients.


Clients will be at different points in their sustainability journey, so you will need to adapt to differing requirements – this may mean greater involvement in sustainability strategy and consultancy with clients whilst also working on reporting strategy over multiple years, setting out a roadmap towards particular goals and frameworks or getting them ready for CSRD for example.


The role may involve managing and mentoring Account Directors on the team as well as leading project teams of analysts, researchers, copywriters, and designers - writing briefs for them and reviewing all work. You’ll be supported by a Project Management Support team as you work with internal teams to produce outstanding sustainability reports and engaging, informative and impactful Sustainability communications including web content, social and other marketing around this.


The successful candidate will need to have had demonstrable experience in consulting or leading the development of sustainability reporting for multiple clients in an agency environment. You will need to have a real passion for sustainability reporting along with a knowledge of latest developments to international legislation / frameworks / compliance. You'll enjoy consulting with clients about their strategy and goals and working with them to create outstanding reports and related communications for their internal and external stakeholders, investors and other target audiences.


This role will require candidates who:


  • Are extremely strong client relationship builders and who work closely and in partnership with internal teams
  • Experienced in leading global clients to deliver an extremely high standard of reporting work and related marketing communications
  • A deep knowledge and passion for sustainability is a must
  • Knowledgeable about international reporting frameworks and standards, such as GRI, IFRS S1 and S2, ESRS (CSRD), TCFD/SASB
  • Are familiar with sustainability materiality assessments and stakeholder engagement processes
  • Have worked with multiple clients at once in different sectors, jurisdictions, with differing requirements
  • Are extremely organised, innovative and have excellent communication skills
  • Enjoy leading project teams and helping more junior colleagues
  • Enjoy leading new business calls / meetings with prospective clients and working on proposals for them.


This role would suit a Senior Account Director who is naturally creative and innovative, and who has extremely high standards whilst paying very close attention to detail. The role involves leading with clients and internal teams so needs someone who takes initiative and is extremely cool under pressure. The Senior Account Director will also need to be confident presenting and excited about working with a variety of clients, projects and a talented and growing agency team with a broad offering.


This role offers an opportunity to make a huge difference for the agency, clients and create work with meaning for some important global clients. It’s working with a highly talented team and offers the opportunity to work remotely for part or all of the working week. Please APPLY NOW or email max@nekolondon.com for more information.

descriptionHtml:
Bradford Jacobs is supporting a UK-based broker-dealer regulated by the FCA, who are expanding from the US. — we are seeking a highly experienced Compliance Officer to assume the dual responsibilities of SMF16 (Compliance Oversight Function) and SMF17 (Money Laundering Reporting Officer).


The firm operates under a non-custodial model and offers a broad range of investment services, including dealing in investments as agent or principal, arranging deals, and placing instruments with institutional investors. The successful candidate will play a critical role in building and maintaining a proportionate and effective compliance infrastructure aligned with FCA requirements and best practices.


Key Responsibilities


Compliance Oversight (SMF16)

  • Serve as the FCA-approved SMF16 (Compliance Oversight Function).
  • Design, implement, and manage a Compliance Monitoring Programme (CMP) suitable for a non-custodial broker-dealer.
  • Ensure full compliance with relevant sections of the FCA Handbook (SYSC, COBS, MAR, PRIN, MLRs).
  • Oversee all regulatory reporting obligations, including REP_CRIM, GABRIEL/REgData filings, and SMCR attestations.
  • Work closely with external legal and regulatory consultants to maintain alignment with FCA expectations.


Advisory & Business Support

  • Provide real-time compliance advisory to internal stakeholders across business functions (e.g., sales, trading, capital markets).
  • Review and advise on new business initiatives, ensuring regulatory requirements are integrated from the outset.
  • Monitor changes in the regulatory environment and ensure timely updates to internal policies and procedures.
  • Lead compliance-related training, including market conduct, AML, and FCA obligations.


Governance & Regulatory Liaison

  • Act as the primary liaison for all FCA interactions, including audits, supervisory meetings, and ad hoc inquiries.
  • Attend board and governance meetings, delivering compliance updates, risk insights, and regulatory findings.
  • Maintain and update core compliance documentation, including manuals, breach logs, and regulatory registers.


Anti-Money Laundering (SMF17)

  • Serve as the firm’s Money Laundering Reporting Officer (MLRO).
  • Oversee AML and financial crime systems and controls, including suspicious activity reporting.
  • Maintain and evolve the firm’s AML framework, including risk assessments, training, and internal controls.


Experience & Expertise

  • Previous FCA-approved SMF16 experience is strongly preferred, ideally within a MiFID investment firm or boutique broker-dealer.
  • 5–7 years of relevant compliance experience within the UK financial services sector.
  • Strong understanding of the FCA regulatory framework, including COBS, MAR, MiFID II, and AML regulations.
  • Proven ability to manage regulatory relationships and interact with senior management and board-level stakeholders.
  • Experience operating in non-custodial models is highly advantageous.
  • CAMS or similar AML qualification is a plus.


Skills & Attributes

  • High level of professional integrity and independent judgment.
  • Strong analytical and communication skills with the ability to influence and advise at all levels.
  • Ability to draft clear, risk-based policies and provide actionable compliance guidance.
  • Comfortable in a fast-paced, entrepreneurial environment with evolving business models.


This is a hybrid model based in London.

Salary range: from £97,000 DOE

Discretionary bonus + benefits.

companyDescription:
Rev & Regs are a specialist recruitment business that place experienced professionals in the areas of:

1) Revenue: This is our team that recruit Revenue Generating/Front Office vacancies (Relationship Managers, Portfolio Managers, Investment Managers, Stock Brokers, FX/CFD Sales, Traders) etc. See website for more details.

2) Regulation: The Regulation team places candidates within (Compliance, Legal, Risk Management, Audit, Financial Crime & Business Change).

Our team are committed to providing tailored recruitment and consulting solutions across the Financial Services and Professional sectors. We place in the UK, EU, USA & ASIA. 

With 16+ years of combined recruiting experience; we have a proven track record in helping our clients attract, engage and hire the best talent in the market. We are proud to have assisted many candidates at all levels of seniority find their ideal position.

We understand that sales and generating revenue are at the core of your businesses but without expert Front Office/Sales Executives, Operations, Governance, Risk & Compliance none of this would be possible. The relationship between these areas is often a challenging landscape, but it is our mission to help our clients hire the best talent, whilst strengthening the relationship between these areas, enabling them to strike the right balance and achieve their objectives.

Our philosophy is simple:

- Be knowledgeable.
- Be trustworthy.
- Deliver against our word.

descriptionHtml:
Rev & Regs are currently recruiting for a 6-month contract Regulatory Reporting Specialist on behalf of a well-established and dynamic broker based in the City of London.


This role offers an excellent opportunity to work within a respected organisation and contribute to critical regulatory reporting functions. If you are available for contract work and have relevant expertise, I would be happy to share further details.


Responsibilities:


  • Responsible for the daily operational support for regulatory transaction reporting for EMIR, and MiFID.
  • Providing day to day Client Report production, quality control and delivery support.
  • Identifying and delivering necessary BAU reporting processes to maintain compliance with an evolving regulatory reporting and business change environment.
  • MIFID SME point for regulatory reporting issues with an aim to identify areas of synergy, scale and efficiencies.
  • Shaping the BAU process for MIFID reporting and reviewing the existing reporting processes and control framework to identify enhancements, as well as maintaining and renewing policies and procedures to comply with reporting obligations.
  • Carrying out remediation work as required when issues are identified across processes and reporting.


Experience:


  • Extensive Regulatory Reporting experience working for a broker.
  • MIFID / EMIR Regulatory Reporting.
  • Preferably you will have knowledge of equity swaps or listed derivatives products.
  • Team player, ability to work flexibly and tenacity.


Pay - £550 - £650 per day

Duration - 6 months (strong possibility of extension)

Location – City of London (4-5 days per week in office)

descriptionHtml:
Company Description

Cardaq UK, established in October 2011, has over 12 years of experience in accepting and processing transparent payments globally. We excel in providing secure and efficient payment solutions, ensuring trust and reliability for our clients. As a leading player in the payment processing industry, we prioritise excellence and transparency in all our operations.


Role Overview

We are seeking a FIU Team Lead – Transaction & Merchant Monitoring to head our internal fraud and AML monitoring team. In this role, you will oversee a small team of 3 analysts focused on both fraud prevention and Anti-Money Laundering (AML) transaction monitoring across our card issuing and acquiring business lines. You will design and enhance monitoring controls, leveraging advanced systems – including Mastercard’s Brighterion AI platform (real-time fraud/AML decisioning), Visa’s Cybersource Decision Manager (fraud detection for acquiring), and Finchecker (AML transaction monitoring) – to and investigate suspicious transactions. This is a hands-on leadership role where you will act as a subject matter expert, guiding complex investigations and ensuring the team’s findings are thorough. You will escalate confirmed issues to the Money Laundering Reporting Officer (MLRO) and work closely with the Head of Risk to protect the business and maintain compliance with regulatory standards.


Key Responsibilities


Team Leadership & Oversight: Lead and mentor the transaction monitoring team (3 analysts). Allocate and prioritize alert reviews, ensuring timely investigation of fraud and AML alerts. Provide guidance on complex cases, review analysts’ work for quality and consistency, and foster a high-performance culture of vigilance and accuracy.


Monitoring Program Management: Oversee daily fraud and AML transaction monitoring across issuing and acquiring channels. Ensure that all system-generated alerts (fraud flags, AML alerts) are addressed promptly and in line with internal procedures. Perform quality assurance on investigations and make sure escalation protocols are followed for high-risk cases.


Controls Design & Enhancement: Design, implement, and continuously improve fraud detection and AML monitoring controls. Develop and tune rules/models within our monitoring platforms to optimize alert effectiveness – adjusting risk scoring thresholds, creating custom rules, and incorporating emerging fraud schemes or money laundering typologies.


Systems Oversight & Implementation: Ensure effective utilization of Cardaq’s monitoring systems (Brighterion, Visa Cybersource, Finchecker). Take ownership of platform configuration and calibration to Cardaq’s specific risk profiles. Lead the transition from legacy systems (such as Decta, which is being phased out) to new tools, coordinating with vendors and IT teams to implement updates, test outputs, and resolve any technical issues.


Investigation & Escalation: Act as the escalation point for complex or significant fraud/AML cases. Personally investigate or review high-priority alerts and cases, ensuring thorough analysis of transaction patterns, customer profiles, and supporting data. Compile clear case narratives and evidence. For confirmed incidents, escalate findings to the MLRO and Head of Risk, and coordinate the preparation of Suspicious Activity Reports (SARs) or other regulatory filings as necessary. Provide recommendations on immediate actions (e.g. blocking transactions, freezing accounts) to mitigate risks.


Reporting: Compile and report on key transaction monitoring metrics and trends to senior management. Prepare regular summaries of fraud/AML incident trends, control performance, and notable cases for the Head of Risk and other stakeholders. Ensure any regulatory reporting (SARs, regulatory returns related to financial crime) is completed accurately and on time in collaboration with the MLRO.


Cross-Functional Collaboration: Work closely with cross-functional teams – including Compliance, Operations, Customer Support, Product, and Technology – to address fraud and AML risks holistically. Coordinate with these teams when investigating suspicious activities (for example, obtaining customer due diligence information from Compliance or transaction details from Operations). Liaise with external parties such as issuing processors, card networks, or law enforcement as needed on fraud/AML cases. Share insights and lessons learned with relevant teams to help improve Cardaq’s products and processes (e.g. advising Product on fraud controls for new features).


Continuous Improvement: Stay up-to-date with the latest financial crime trends, card scheme bulletins, and regulatory developments in fraud and AML. Proactively identify new patterns or typologies that could affect Cardaq’s business, and ensure the monitoring team adapts accordingly. Drive updates to internal policies, procedures, and training based on investigation findings and industry best practices. Champion a culture of continuous improvement and vigilance in the fight against financial crime.


Required Qualifications & Experience


·      Education: Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Business, Criminal Justice, or a related field (or equivalent work experience). Professional certifications such as ACAMS or ICA (Anti-Money Laundering/Compliance certifications) are highly desirable.


·      Experience: 5+ years of experience in transaction monitoring, fraud prevention, and AML compliance within financial services (preferably in fintech, payments, or banking). This must include hands-on investigation of both fraud cases and AML alerts. Leadership experience (e.g. 1–3 years as a team lead or supervisor in a financial crime/fraud monitoring function) is required, with a track record of effectively managing and developing analysts.


·      Systems Knowledge: Strong proficiency with automated fraud detection and AML transaction monitoring systems. Direct experience with platforms such as Brighterion, Visa Cybersource Decision Manager, and Finchecker (or similar tools) is required. Ability to adjust system parameters, write or modify detection rules, and interpret machine-learning model outputs. Familiarity with legacy card monitoring systems (e.g. Decta or comparable platforms) is a plus, as you will oversee system upgrades and migrations.


·      Regulatory Knowledge: In-depth understanding of relevant AML laws and regulations (e.g. UK Money Laundering Regulations, Proceeds of Crime Act) and knowledge of card network (Visa/Mastercard) fraud rules and compliance programs. Experience with regulatory obligations such as SAR reporting and handling law enforcement inquiries. Keeps abreast of regulatory changes and ensures team compliance with all requirements.


·      Analytical Skills: Exceptional analytical and problem-solving abilities. Comfortable working with large datasets of transactions to identify anomalies or patterns indicative of fraud or money laundering. Able to quickly assess complex situations and synthesize data from multiple sources to make informed decisions on risk.


·      Communication: Excellent written and verbal communication skills. Capable of drafting clear, concise investigative reports and SAR narratives that can stand up to regulatory scrutiny. Able to present investigation findings and risk insights to senior management in a confident, structured manner. Strong interpersonal skills to communicate directives and guidance to the team, and to collaborate with other departments.


·      Organization: Proven people management and organizational skills. Able to manage the team’s workflow and multiple investigations simultaneously while meeting deadlines and maintaining high quality. Demonstrates a high level of integrity, handles sensitive information with discretion, and leads by example in fostering a compliance-focused culture.


Desired Skills & Competencies


·      Technical Proficiency: Advanced skills in data analysis tools such as Excel; ability to utilize pivot tables, VLOOKUPs, and data analytics functions to dissect transaction data. Experience with SQL or other data query languages is an advantage. Familiarity with machine learning or AI concepts in fraud detection is a plus (as our monitoring platforms employ AI/ML for decisioning).


·      Critical & Strategic Thinking: Thinks both like a fraudster and a compliance officer – able to anticipate potential fraud schemes or loopholes and identify control weaknesses before they are exploited. Offers a proactive mindset in suggesting new rules, controls, or process improvements to address emerging risks or gaps in the monitoring program.


·      Adaptability: Thrives in a fast-paced fintech environment. Adaptable to evolving tools, regulatory expectations, and business models. Quick to learn new systems or technologies related to transaction monitoring. Comfortable managing change, such as updates in card network rules or the introduction of new products, and adjusting the team’s processes accordingly.


·      Collaborative Team Player: A leader who is still a team player – works effectively within a small team and across departments. Approachable and supportive, with a willingness to mentor and develop team members. Able to build strong working relationships with colleagues in Compliance, Operations, IT, etc., to jointly combat fraud and financial crime.


·      Professionalism: Self-motivated and results-driven with a strong sense of responsibility. Maintains professionalism under pressure, especially when responding to incidents or interacting with external bodies. Takes a proactive approach to enhancing the company’s risk posture and protecting customers, acting as a role model for ethical behaviour and diligence in the team.


Benefits


Cardaq offers a comprehensive benefits package including private healthcare, pension contribution, paid holidays, and professional development support. We also provide flexibility through our hybrid work model and other wellness perks.

Professional certifications such as ACAMS or ICA (Anti-Money Laundering/Compliance certifications) are highly desirable.·      Experience: 5+ years of experience in transaction monitoring, fraud prevention, and AML compliance within financial services (preferably in fintech, payments, or banking). This must include hands-on investigation of both fraud cases and AML alerts. Leadership experience (e.g. 1–3 years as a team lead or supervisor in a financial crime/fraud monitoring function) is required, with a track record of effectively managing and developing analysts.·      Systems Knowledge: Strong proficiency with automated fraud detection and AML transaction monitoring systems. Direct experience with platforms such as Brighterion, Visa Cybersource Decision Manager, and Finchecker (or similar tools) is required. Ability to adjust system parameters, write or modify detection rules, and interpret machine-learning model outputs. Familiarity with legacy card monitoring systems (e.g. Decta or comparable platforms) is a plus, as you will oversee system upgrades and migrations.·      Regulatory Knowledge: In-depth understanding of relevant AML laws and regulations (e.g. UK Money Laundering Regulations, Proceeds of Crime Act) and knowledge of card network (Visa/Mastercard) fraud rules and compliance programs. Experience with regulatory obligations such as SAR reporting and handling law enforcement inquiries. Keeps abreast of regulatory changes and ensures team compliance with all requirements.·      Analytical Skills: Exceptional analytical and problem-solving abilities. Comfortable working with large datasets of transactions to identify anomalies or patterns indicative of fraud or money laundering. Able to quickly assess complex situations and synthesize data from multiple sources to make informed decisions on risk.·      Communication: Excellent written and verbal communication skills. Capable of drafting clear, concise investigative reports and SAR narratives that can stand up to regulatory scrutiny. Able to present investigation findings and risk insights to senior management in a confident, structured manner. Strong interpersonal skills to communicate directives and guidance to the team, and to collaborate with other departments.·      Organization: Proven people management and organizational skills. Able to manage the team’s workflow and multiple investigations simultaneously while meeting deadlines and maintaining high quality. Demonstrates a high level of integrity, handles sensitive information with discretion, and leads by example in fostering a compliance-focused culture.Desired Skills & Competencies·      Technical Proficiency: Advanced skills in data analysis tools such as Excel; ability to utilize pivot tables, VLOOKUPs, and data analytics functions to dissect transaction data. Experience with SQL or other data query languages is an advantage. Familiarity with machine learning or AI concepts in fraud detection is a plus (as our monitoring platforms employ AI/ML for decisioning).·      Critical & Strategic Thinking: Thinks both like a fraudster and a compliance officer – able to anticipate potential fraud schemes or loopholes and identify control weaknesses before they are exploited. Offers a proactive mindset in suggesting new rules, controls, or process improvements to address emerging risks or gaps in the monitoring program.·      Adaptability: Thrives in a fast-paced fintech environment. Adaptable to evolving tools, regulatory expectations, and business models. Quick to learn new systems or technologies related to transaction monitoring. Comfortable managing change, such as updates in card network rules or the introduction of new products, and adjusting the team’s processes accordingly.·      Collaborative Team Player: A leader who is still a team player – works effectively within a small team and across departments. Approachable and supportive, with a willingness to mentor and develop team members. Able to build strong working relationships with colleagues in Compliance, Operations, IT, etc., to jointly combat fraud and financial crime.·      Professionalism: Self-motivated and results-driven with a strong sense of responsibility. Maintains professionalism under pressure, especially when responding to incidents or interacting with external bodies. Takes a proactive approach to enhancing the company’s risk posture and protecting customers, acting as a role model for ethical behaviour and diligence in the team.BenefitsCardaq offers a comprehensive benefits package including private healthcare, pension contribution, paid holidays, and professional development support. We also provide flexibility through our hybrid work model and other wellness perks.
title: FIU Team Lead (Transaction & Merchant Monitoring)

descriptionHtml:
Role: Quality Assurance Assistant

Location: Thirsk, YO7


Our client is a global leader in food, agriculture, and industrial solutions, with a strong commitment to nourishing the world in a safe, responsible, and sustainable way. Operating across more than 70 countries with over 160,000 employees worldwide, the business connects farmers with markets, customers with high-quality ingredients, and communities with essential products that impact billions of lives every day.

This role sits within the Animal Nutrition and Health division, which is focused on improving animal lives through better nutrition. The team is dedicated to research, innovation, and creating solutions that deliver healthy and sustainable results for customers.

Job Purpose and Impact

The Quality Assurance Assistant will support the FSQR Manager in daily activities to ensure compliance with regulatory requirements, accreditation standards, company feed safety policies, and customer expectations.


Key Accountabilities

  • Manage and maintain the testing surveillance program, including interpretation of results and resolution of non-conformance issues.
  • Support production in assessing and approving raw materials for positive release.
  • Provide relevant data for KPI reporting.
  • Assist in investigations related to non-conformance and customer complaints, applying root cause analysis and monitoring preventive actions.
  • Act as Deputy for the FSQR Manager when required.
  • Promote food and product safety awareness across the site, helping to develop the competencies of plant professionals.
  • Support training, coaching, and mentoring of team members on food and product safety procedures.
  • Assist in updating specific Quality Management System (QMS) procedures.
  • Contribute to internal and external safety and quality audits, helping to close gaps in production and maintenance processes.
  • Handle routine issues with supervision, escalating more complex matters as necessary.
  • Carry out other duties as assigned.


Requirements

Minimum:

  • Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field, or equivalent experience.
  • Experience using Microsoft Office applications and SAP.
  • Practical laboratory experience (e.g., NIR, mycotoxin testing equipment).

Preferred:

  • Previous experience in a food manufacturing environment.
  • Exposure to third-party audits (e.g., UFAS, VDM, Local Authority).


For more information please contact Travis Townsend at Major Recruitment.

descriptionHtml:
Are you an experienced risk and compliance professional looking for a role where you can truly make an impact? Do you want to be part of a growing law firm that values your expertise where you can work closely with senior leadership to shape a strong compliance culture? If so, we’d love to hear from you.

Reporting to the Risk and Compliance Partner, this role is integral to ensuring the firm operates ethically, efficiently, and in full compliance with regulatory requirements. You’ll play a key role in managing risk, developing policies, and embedding a strong compliance culture across the firm.


About Us

At Dutton Gregory, we pride ourselves on innovation, collaboration, and delivering exceptional client service. As we grow, maintaining a strong risk and compliance framework is crucial to our continued success.


The Role

As our Risk & Compliance Manager, you will:

  • Ensure compliance with SRA standards and regulations, AML requirements, GDPR, and other legal obligations.
  • Develop, implement, and monitor risk and compliance policies and procedures.
  • Conduct audits, risk assessments, and compliance checks, providing recommendations for improvement.
  • Deliver training and guidance to employees on regulatory responsibilities.
  • Support the Risk & Compliance Partner with client due diligence, AML checks, and risk management processes.
  • Work closely with senior leadership to drive a culture of compliance and best practice.


About You

We’re looking for someone who has:

  • Experience in a risk and compliance role within a professional law firm.
  • Strong knowledge of SRA regulations, AML and data protection laws.
  • Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills.
  • A proactive, detail-oriented, and collaborative approach.

 

Why Join Us?

When you join Dutton Gregory, you’ll benefit from:

  • A supportive and inclusive workplace culture where creativity and innovation are encouraged.
  • Opportunities for professional development and career growth.
  • A key role in a firm with strong growth ambitions and a dynamic culture.

descriptionHtml:
Your new company
A leading organisation in the legal sector who are recruiting as they continue to expand their back office operation.
Your new role
This is a hugely varied role where you will pick up a hugely varied workload. Your key duties will include:

  • Manage relationships with landlords, oversee maintenance, furnishings, inventory, procurement, and physical security.
  • Supervise reception and cleaning staff, support space planning, internal moves, and lease-related processes.
  • Oversee outsourced IT provider, manage IT procurement, telecoms, and act as secondary authority on IT policy and security.
  • Act as deputy to COO in disaster recovery, business continuity, and data breach scenarios.
  • Support recruitment, onboarding, appraisals, contract administration, HR records, and benefits.
  • Manage service contracts and procurement activities, including maintenance, cleaning, and reception services.
  • Assist with budgeting, forecasting, financial reporting, and deputise in banking and audit relationships.
  • Administer telecoms and mobile contracts, and ensure smooth operation of IT and office equipment.
  • Assist with policy development, compliance tracking, and process documentation.
  • Prepare management reports, support internal committees, and assist the COO with regulatory and compliance work.

What you'll need to succeed

  • Proven experience in facilities, IT, HR, or operations management within a legal or professional services environment.
  • Strong organisational and multitasking skills, with the ability to manage service providers, contracts, and compliance processes effectively.
  • Demonstrated ability to support senior leadership, including deputising in areas such as business continuity, financial oversight, and regulatory matters.
  • Excellent interpersonal and communication skills, with experience liaising with landlords, outsourced providers, and internal stakeholders.
  • Proficient in managing IT systems, HR databases, and financial reporting tools, with a proactive approach to problem-solving and continuous improvement.

What you'll get in return


What you need to do now


If you're interested in this role, click 'apply now' to forward an up-to-date copy of your CV, or call us now.
If this job isn't quite right for you, but you are looking for a new position, please contact us for a confidential discussion about your career.

descriptionHtml:
Financial Services client is hiring for a Regulatory Reporting Manager with London Market experience for their team based in London. This is a permanent role based in the City, working on a hybrid basis (2-3 days). Salary on offer is between £90K - £110K + Bonus + Benefits. You will take ownership of the preparation, review, and delivery of key regulatory reporting requirements across their Insurance business. This role is central to ensuring accuracy, compliance, and timely submission of all monthly, quarterly, and annual returns, while also driving process improvements and supporting a high-performing offshore team.


Responsibilities:

  • Prepare and peer-review all required regulatory returns (monthly, quarterly, and annual) in line with compliance regulations.
  • Ensure strong adherence to Solvency II, Bermuda Monetary Authority (BMA), and NAIC reporting requirements.
  • Manage, oversee, and review the workload of a team of 8–10 offshore staff, including transition of regulatory reporting activities.
  • Stay current with evolving regulatory requirements and lead the timely implementation of updates across policies, procedures, and reporting.
  • Produce clear, well-documented workpapers and maintain detailed desktop procedures in compliance with SOX and internal standards.
  • Identify and recommend process improvements to streamline reporting, enhance accuracy, and increase efficiency.
  • Oversee the adoption and implementation of process automation tools and technologies.
  • Build deep knowledge of internal systems and tools to enhance reporting capabilities.
  • Respond to internal and external queries regarding submitted financial data.


Skills and Experience:

  • Professional Accountancy Qualification (ACA/ACCA) or Master’s degree in Accountancy.
  • At least 10 years’ experience in London Market regulatory reporting.
  • In-depth knowledge and hands-on experience in preparing and reviewing Solvency II, BMA, and NAIC returns.
  • Exceptional attention to detail, accuracy, and strong analytical ability.
  • High proficiency in Microsoft Office applications.
  • Strong communication and organisational skills, with experience engaging peers, clients, and senior stakeholders.


Please apply for immediate interview!

companyDescription:
At American Express, we know that with the right backing, people and businesses have the power to progress in incredible ways. Whether we’re supporting our customers’ financial confidence to move ahead, taking commerce to new heights, or encouraging people to explore the world, our colleagues are constantly striving to uphold our powerful backing promise to our customers and each other every day. 
 
These beliefs have been our North Star for 170 years as our business transformed – from helping evacuate travelers during World Wars, to ensuring the safety of our customers’ funds during the Great Depression in the U.S., to creating the Shop Small® movement to help small businesses recover from the Financial Crisis, to providing aid to communities impacted by many natural disasters and so much more.  
 
For generations, the key to our success has been the determination and resilience of our American Express colleagues. Now, as a globally integrated payments company, we work together to provide customers with access to products, insights and world-class experiences that enrich lives and build business success. Join us and let’s lead the way together.
