descriptionHtml:
At American Express, our culture is built on a 175-year history of innovation, shared values and Leadership Behaviors, and an unwavering commitment to back our customers, communities, and colleagues. As part of Team Amex, you'll experience this powerful backing with comprehensive support for your holistic well-being and many opportunities to learn new skills, develop as a leader, and grow your career.

Here, your voice and ideas matter, your work makes an impact, and together, you will help us define the future of American Express.:


The Demand Generation team sits within UK Acquisition and Marketing as part of the International Card Services organization.


Commercial acquisition is a dynamic, fast-growing area of the business as we continue to lead and innovate in the commercial payments solutions space within the UK. The team are responsible for generating demand for our B2B products which help small, mid-size, and large corporations around the world manage nearly every facet of their business spending. 


This position is an excellent opportunity for a passionate, forward-thinking individual looking to expand their marketing skills. The role will focus on our Above the Line (ATL) campaigns to help us meet our goals of driving upper funnel metrics (awareness and consideration) alongside NAA (New accounts acquired) to drive growth. 

With the support of a B35, the marketing analyst will be responsible for the end-to-end delivery of our campaigns in collaboration with colleagues from Marketing, Brand, Consumer, Product, Sales and PR as well as our external creative and media agencies. The role will cover a breadth of channels (TV, OOH, Radio, Digital, Social and Site).


Key Responsibilities:

  • Plan and deliver successful, innovative, ATL campaigns to drive our upper funnel metrics of awareness and consideration, for Amex Business, whilst driving quality traffic to site to sustainably grow our base.

  • Manage the day-to-day communications of the campaign process with our key agencies and partners.

  • Lead on end-to-end project statuses including updates on timings, finance, IMH and delivery, ensuring we stay on track and meet SLAs.

  • Adhere to the highest standards of business conduct to ensure we exceed regulatory and compliance expectations in all our communications.

  • Lead on campaign measurement, reporting weekly to B40 level when we are live on media and site metrics and communicating the trends and performance back to our wider stakeholders. Explore optimisations we can make to drive better results.

  • Administration of campaign budgets, including raising requisitions, processing invoices, vendor liaison and internal reconciliation.

Minimum Qualifications:


The successful candidate will be highly professional, confident, and personable with high levels of energy, and a proactive attitude. The candidate will need to be organised to work in a fast-paced environment and able to manage multiple projects and deadlines, prioritising effectively. It is essential that the candidate is a team player, able to work collaboratively with a variety of internal and external teams, as well as having the ability to drive projects independently.


Key requirements:

  • A passion for marketing and ability to demonstrate an understanding of marketing principles

  • Strong relationship management skills

  • Creative problem-solving, and ability to identify and drive customized solutions

  • Outstanding verbal and written communication skills

  • Good analytical skills, with ability to identify key success drivers for campaigns

  • Ability to work under pressure and meet tight deadlines

  • Exceptional attention to detail and strong organisational skills

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Previous experience in acquisition and/or ATL marketing
 

Employment eligibility to work with American Express in the UK is required as the company will not pursue visa sponsorship for these positions. 

 




We back you with benefits that support your holistic well-being so you can be and deliver your best. This means caring for you and your loved ones' physical, financial, and mental health, as well as providing the flexibility you need to thrive personally and professionally:

  • Competitive base salaries 

  • Bonus incentives 

  • Support for financial-well-being and retirement 

  • Comprehensive medical, dental, vision, life insurance, and disability benefits (depending on location) 

  • Flexible working model with hybrid, onsite or virtual arrangements depending on role and business need 

  • Generous paid parental leave policies (depending on your location) 

  • Free access to global on-site wellness centers staffed with nurses and doctors (depending on location) 

  • Free and confidential counseling support through our Healthy Minds program 

  • Career development and training opportunities
Offer of employment with American Express is conditioned upon the successful completion of a background verification check, subject to applicable laws and regulations.

The team are responsible for generating demand for our B2B products which help small, mid-size, and large corporations around the world manage nearly every facet of their business spending. This position is an excellent opportunity for a passionate, forward-thinking individual looking to expand their marketing skills. The role will focus on our Above the Line (ATL) campaigns to help us meet our goals of driving upper funnel metrics (awareness and consideration) alongside NAA (New accounts acquired) to drive growth. With the support of a B35, the marketing analyst will be responsible for the end-to-end delivery of our campaigns in collaboration with colleagues from Marketing, Brand, Consumer, Product, Sales and PR as well as our external creative and media agencies. The role will cover a breadth of channels (TV, OOH, Radio, Digital, Social and Site).Key Responsibilities:Plan and deliver successful, innovative, ATL campaigns to drive our upper funnel metrics of awareness and consideration, for Amex Business, whilst driving quality traffic to site to sustainably grow our base.Manage the day-to-day communications of the campaign process with our key agencies and partners.Lead on end-to-end project statuses including updates on timings, finance, IMH and delivery, ensuring we stay on track and meet SLAs.Adhere to the highest standards of business conduct to ensure we exceed regulatory and compliance expectations in all our communications.Lead on campaign measurement, reporting weekly to B40 level when we are live on media and site metrics and communicating the trends and performance back to our wider stakeholders. Explore optimisations we can make to drive better results.Administration of campaign budgets, including raising requisitions, processing invoices, vendor liaison and internal reconciliation.Minimum Qualifications:The successful candidate will be highly professional, confident, and personable with high levels of energy, and a proactive attitude. The candidate will need to be organised to work in a fast-paced environment and able to manage multiple projects and deadlines, prioritising effectively. It is essential that the candidate is a team player, able to work collaboratively with a variety of internal and external teams, as well as having the ability to drive projects independently.Key requirements:A passion for marketing and ability to demonstrate an understanding of marketing principlesStrong relationship management skillsCreative problem-solving, and ability to identify and drive customized solutionsOutstanding verbal and written communication skillsGood analytical skills, with ability to identify key success drivers for campaignsAbility to work under pressure and meet tight deadlinesExceptional attention to detail and strong organisational skillsPreferred Qualifications:Previous experience in acquisition and/or ATL marketing Employment eligibility to work with American Express in the UK is required as the company will not pursue visa sponsorship for these positions.  We back you with benefits that support your holistic well-being so you can be and deliver your best. This means caring for you and your loved ones' physical, financial, and mental health, as well as providing the flexibility you need to thrive personally and professionally:Competitive base salaries Bonus incentives Support for financial-well-being and retirement Comprehensive medical, dental, vision, life insurance, and disability benefits (depending on location) Flexible working model with hybrid, onsite or virtual arrangements depending on role and business need Generous paid parental leave policies (depending on your location) Free access to global on-site wellness centers staffed with nurses and doctors (depending on location) Free and confidential counseling support through our Healthy Minds program Career development and training opportunitiesOffer of employment with American Express is conditioned upon the successful completion of a background verification check, subject to applicable laws and regulations.
title: Marketing Analyst

descriptionHtml:
Role

 JOB DESCRIPTION

As a member of the Group Compliance Team contributing to the maintenance of a robust Compliance Framework and annual Compliance Plan which collectively identify, assess, and monitor legal & regulatory risk, and facilitate the escalation / reporting of relevant matters.

Responsibilities

  • Assist the Compliance Team in the delivery of the Annual Compliance Monitoring Plan, via a combination of Focused, Thematic, and Awareness type quality assurance reviews.
  • Support the evolution of the Compliance Universe & Data Protection framework, including the ongoing review of key risk & performance metrics.
  • Coordinate the administration of Compliance Function activities, and support each of the compliance pillars (Financial Crime / Compliance / Data Protection) in maintaining a proper log / audit trail of activities.
  • Assist in the coordination of bespoke compliance sponsored workshops and training programs to enhance business awareness of key legislative & regulatory matters.
  • Assist in the ongoing maintenance of compliance owned Policies, Processes, and Procedures across all global entities.
  • Assist the compliance pillars in the monitoring of business activities against internal and external requirements, reporting MI as necessary to demonstrate effective oversight.
  • Coordinate Compliance Team meetings, Action Logs, and assist team members in maintaining structured records of business activities.
  • Ad hoc project and transactional work

Skills And Experience

Attention to detail with a high degree of accuracy in work product

Collaborative team worker

Approachable, open minded and logical

Willingness to help innovate and drive change where required

Ability to work well under pressure and unphased by competing priorities, deadlines and volumes of work

Our Benefits

 ABOUT US

We offer all employees a comprehensive benefits package that focuses on their whole wellbeing. This includes hybrid working, a competitive base salary, non-contributory pension, discretionary bonus, insurances including health (family) and dental cover, and many other benefits to enhance financial, physical, social and psychological health.

About Canopius

Canopius is a global specialty lines (re)insurer. We are one of the leading insurers in the Lloyd’s of London insurance market with offices in the UK, US, Singapore, Australia and Bermuda.

At Canopius we foster a distinctive, positive culture which enables us to bring our whole selves to work to flourish as people, and build a business which delivers profitable, sustainable results.

Based in incredible new offices in the heart of the City of London, Canopius operates a flexible, hybrid working model and is committed to providing an environment that challenges employees to be their best and where everyone's unique contributions are recognised, valued and respected.

We are fully committed to equal employment opportunities for all applicants and providing employees with a work environment free of discrimination and harassment. All employment decisions are made regardless of age, sex, gender identity, ethnicity, disability, sexual orientation, socio-economic background, religion or beliefs, marital or caring status, or any other status protected by the laws or regulations in the locations where we operate. We encourage and welcome applicants from all diverse backgrounds.

We make reasonable adjustments throughout the recruitment process and during employment. Please let us know if you require any information in an alternate format or any other reasonable adjustments.

descriptionHtml:
Ledbury, Herefordshire

37.5hrs per week

Are you a Life Sciences graduate with a sharp eye for detail and a passion for quality? We're looking for a motivated individual to join our team as an Auditor. While experience in Good Laboratory Practice (GLP) auditing is a plus, full training will be provided.

If you're detail-oriented, a strong communicator, and eager to contribute to a supportive team, this is an excellent opportunity to build a rewarding career in Quality Assurance.

Who We Are

Sequani is a contract research organisation based in the beautiful Herefordshire countryside. We conduct regulatory safety testing to help make the world a safer place.

As a Gold Investors in People organisation, we are confident that we can offer a varied role, together with a good range of company benefits and excellent career progression. Our customers frequently complement us on our friendly, professional, and high-quality service and we inject commitment and purpose into everything we do.

Our Quality Assurance (QA) team plays a vital role in ensuring that all procedures, facilities, and studies meet the highest standards of Good Laboratory Practice (GLP). As an independent function, QA is not directly involved in conducting the studies, which allows the team to provide objective oversight.

Role Overview

As part of our team, you will play a key role in ensuring the integrity and compliance of studies conducted at Sequani Limited. This involves monitoring study conduct and reporting, as well as inspecting critical study phases, procedures, facilities, and systems. You will also carry out audits of SOPs, study plans, and reports, applying your knowledge and developing expertise as you progress.

What We're Looking For

The ideal candidate will have a genuine enthusiasm for quality, a strong desire to learn and develop, and a dependable, conscientious approach to their work.

You must be a graduate in a life science or have relevant experience and its advantageous to have had some work experience in a preclinical environment.

You'll have a keen eye for detail, able to focus on your own tasks while also contributing to the team's goals. Strong communication skills and a supportive, flexible approach are essential, as early starts and late finishes may be required.

What We Can Offer You

We are a small friendly company with an unrivalled focus on Quality and Customer service. We pride ourselves in offering competitive salaries and benefits. We offer a Health cash plan, including 24hr GP support line. A life insurance policy for 4x your annual salary, as well as local and countrywide corporate gym memberships. We also have an employee discount scheme to cover all your shopping and holiday discounts needs.

We can offer you first class training to support you in your career development, whether you already have experience or whether this is your first professional role

If you're detail orientated, willing to learn and excited by the idea of contributing to meaningful scientific work, we'd love to hear from you.

We reserve the right to close this vacancy early if we receive a high volume of applications. We encourage you to apply as soon as possible to ensure your application is considered.

LNKD1_UKTJ

descriptionHtml:
QHSE & Commercial Compliance Coordinator


Location - Hybrid (flex) Leicestershire

Contract - Perm role part time (3 days a week)


We are looking for a detail-oriented coordinator to lead the development and upkeep of company policies, procedures, and compliance frameworks. This role is key to ensuring safety, regulatory alignment, and commercial governance across our operations.


Key Responsibilities

  • Policy & Process Development: Standardise and maintain policies, SOPs, and training guides in collaboration with department leads.
  • QHSE Oversight: Manage H&S documentation, support ISO standards, assist with audits, and drive continual improvement.
  • Commercial Compliance: Oversee insurance registers, contracts, risk assessments, and client compliance requirements.
  • Governance & Authority Matrix: Define decision rights, maintain controls, and train staff on compliance protocols.
  • IT Security & Systems: Manage SharePoint permissions, ensure secure data handling, and support cybersecurity best practices.
  • Documentation Management: Maintain structured document libraries, ensure version control, and provide compliance reporting.


Qualifications

  • Experience in logistics, warehouse, or engineering environments.
  • Knowledge of ISO (9001, 14001, 45001) and audits.
  • Skilled in policy writing, SOPs, and document control.
  • Proficient in Microsoft 365 and SharePoint.
  • NEBOSH/IOSH or equivalent H&S knowledge desirable.
  • Familiarity with insurance, contracts, and commercial compliance.


Personal Attributes

  • Detail-Oriented with strong accuracy in documentation.
  • Independent & Proactive, with excellent time management.
  • Collaborative Communicator who builds trust across teams.
  • Systematic & Process-Focused, with a drive for improvement.
  • Trustworthy & Discreet with compliance data.

Job Title: Medical Information Manager – German Speaking

Location: Remote / EU-based

Working Hours: 8:00 AM – 4:00 PM


Are you an experienced Medical Information professional fluent in German and English? We’re hiring a Medical Information Manager to support global and country-level medical teams in delivering accurate, timely, and compliant responses to escalated medical enquiries. This role offers an exciting opportunity to contribute to the development and review of medical information content, support digital initiatives, and generate strategic insights.


Key Responsibilities:

  • Provide expert responses to complex medical enquiries from country affiliates within set KPIs and quality standards.
  • Review and write scientifically balanced, evidence-based medical information documents.
  • Ensure compliance with medical, regulatory, legal, and safety requirements.
  • Collaborate on the development and tactical planning of medical information deliverables.
  • Support reporting and analytics on enquiry trends and insights.
  • Contribute to digital content strategy and process improvement initiatives.


Requirements:

  • Minimum of 3 years' experience in Medical Information, ideally with both local and global exposure.
  • Degree in a healthcare-related field (advanced degree such as PhD, PharmD, or MD preferred).
  • Fluency in German and English (written and spoken).
  • Strong attention to detail, communication skills, and ability to manage complex tasks.
  • Proficiency in optimizing workflows and collaborating across global teams.

descriptionHtml:
Our client, a MEDIA/MARKETING business based near ST PAULS STATION, is seeking someone to join their team as a AUDIT/COMPLIANCE ANALYST. This is an incredible time to join this amazing business who really value their staff.


This pivotal role supports the Commercial Compliance Director in ensuring the agency fully adheres to its contractual obligations. A core focus is collaborating with the commercial team to manage charging mechanisms and client notifications with precision and timeliness. Equally important is strengthening systems, controls, and processes to safeguard against revenue loss and uphold operational integrity.


RESPONSIBILITIES

  • Contractual Oversight: Ensure accurate interpretation and reporting of the company's contractual commitments, including coordination with affiliate entities.
  • Commercial-Finance Alignment: Act as a bridge between commercial and finance teams to apply correct charging methodologies across all income streams.
  • Governance & Compliance: Support the Commercial Compliance Director in validating new commercial models and strengthening governance frameworks.
  • Audit & Analysis: Coordinate client audits, deliver ad hoc commercial analysis, and ensure timely, accurate data provision to third-party auditors.
  • Process & Controls: Maintain and update the compliance schedule, review control processes, and recommend improvements to mitigate revenue risk.
  • Income Assurance: Analyse transactional processes and client contractual terms to ensure all income opportunities are identified and captured


SKILLS REQUIRED

This role will suit someone who is currently working as an auditor in a top 20 practice


  • Degree in Business or equivalent professional experience within a finance function
  • Solid understanding of accounting principles and their practical implementation
  • Outstanding verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to engage professionally across all levels of an organisation
  • Skilled in reviewing and interpreting client contractual terms
  • Comfortable working under pressure and meeting tight deadlines
  • Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel
  • Familiarity with the media and communications sector; direct industry experience preferred but not essential


APPLY TODAY FOR THIS GREAT ROLE!!!

descriptionHtml:
Contract Type Permanent Location London The Vacancy

TPT Investment Management (TPTIM) are seeking a highly-organised and detail-oriented Reporting and Data Analyst to help drive our client reporting and analytics function.

The role will encompass development of the reporting and analytics capability across DB, DC and our newly announced CDC (Collective DC) pensions proposition. We are looking for a driven and enthusiastic reporting and data analyst to join our growing team. TPTIM are an investment management company which serves a variety of pension fund clients. We are innovative and use the industry-leading investment technology like Charles River Investment Management System and MSCI BarraOne along with Microsoft Fabric for our cloud data team. TPTIM invest in a wide range of asset classes from equities and bonds to private credit, infrastructure and real estate.

You will have the opportunity to help drive our reporting and data analytics capability and learn about the investment process from front to back. You dont need to necessarily have pensions or financial services experience but you will need to be a quick learner and rapidly gain skills and knowledge.

You Will Be Responsible For

  • Collating and managing TPTIMs data sets.
  • Designing, implementing & developing cloud-based data processes.
  • Ensuring accurate and timely client reporting on performance, risk & attribution.
  • Manage and review performance calculation processes across all asset classes, including benchmarks and custom indices.
  • Reviewing performance calculations and working with third-party vendors.
  • Lead or support process improvement initiatives and contribute to automation efforts.
  • Provide cover to investment operations workflows, including trade support, reconciliations, cash management, and corporate actions.
  • Work with custodians, fund administrators, and other service providers to resolve operational and data issues.
  • Ensuring governance, compliance, and audit support.
  • Managing relationships with data providers and resolving data issues.
  • Championing team values and supporting a collaborative work environment.

Skills & Experience

The ideal candidate will have:

  • Strong experience in investment performance reporting and attribution
  • Good technical skills around data compilation, analysis and dissemination.
  • A working knowledge of the UK pension fund landscape including regulatory reporting or multi-asset strategies would be ideal.
  • The ability to identify and drive change in an organisation and experience of helping to define and implement strategy.
  • Technical skills in Python or similar programming/scripting, and database or data warehouse experience.
  • Advanced Excel analysis and reporting tool skills (e.g. FactSet, Bloomberg PORT).
  • Excellent communication and stakeholder management.

TPT Investment Management operates a hybrid working model with 2 days per week in the office.

We are a team that behave like a startup; leveraging new technology, ways of working and speed to market. If youre a dynamic, driven, data-oriented person then wed love to hear from you

The Company

TPT Retirement Solutions (TPT) is one of the UKs leading providers of workplace pensions with over 75 years experience of managing defined benefit and defined contribution pension schemes. It has £11.1 billion of assets under management (as at 30 September 2024) and 470,000 members.

TPTs mission is to make pension schemes perform better for everyone, from the sponsoring employers and trustees to the members who are saving for the future. They are an innovative, forward-thinking organisation, investing in technology to improve the services they provide. TPT are ultimately owned by a pension fund, providing genuine alignment of interests with those of their clients and scheme members.

Benefits

Competitive salary

Performance-related bonus

Pension scheme

Sports and social events

Training and development plan

Flu jabs

You dont need to necessarily have pensions or financial services experience but you will need to be a quick learner and rapidly gain skills and knowledge.You Will Be Responsible ForCollating and managing TPTIMs data sets.Designing, implementing & developing cloud-based data processes.Ensuring accurate and timely client reporting on performance, risk & attribution.Manage and review performance calculation processes across all asset classes, including benchmarks and custom indices.Reviewing performance calculations and working with third-party vendors.Lead or support process improvement initiatives and contribute to automation efforts.Provide cover to investment operations workflows, including trade support, reconciliations, cash management, and corporate actions.Work with custodians, fund administrators, and other service providers to resolve operational and data issues.Ensuring governance, compliance, and audit support.Managing relationships with data providers and resolving data issues.Championing team values and supporting a collaborative work environment.Skills & ExperienceThe ideal candidate will have:Strong experience in investment performance reporting and attributionGood technical skills around data compilation, analysis and dissemination.A working knowledge of the UK pension fund landscape including regulatory reporting or multi-asset strategies would be ideal.The ability to identify and drive change in an organisation and experience of helping to define and implement strategy.Technical skills in Python or similar programming/scripting, and database or data warehouse experience.Advanced Excel analysis and reporting tool skills (e.g. FactSet, Bloomberg PORT).Excellent communication and stakeholder management.TPT Investment Management operates a hybrid working model with 2 days per week in the office.We are a team that behave like a startup; leveraging new technology, ways of working and speed to market. descriptionHtml:
Compliance Specialist – International Law Firm London | Permanent | Full-Time


An international law firm with a strong presence across Europe and the Middle East is seeking a Compliance Specialist to join its London-based team. This role plays a key part in supporting client onboarding and compliance processes across multiple jurisdictions, with a particular focus on Anti-Money Laundering (AML), Client Due Diligence (CDD), and sanctions screening.


Please note: Fluency in both French and English is essential for this role.


Key Responsibilities:

  • Facilitate onboarding of new clients, matters, and third-party payors in line with AML, CDD, and sanctions policies
  • Review client intake forms and screening reports to ensure regulatory compliance
  • Maintain accurate records of client identification and verification
  • Monitor regulatory changes and assist with policy updates across relevant jurisdictions
  • Conduct training for attorneys and staff on compliance procedures
  • Support internal monitoring and reporting processes
  • Participate in testing and implementation of compliance-related software tools
  • Assist with ad hoc compliance projects and provide cover during team absences


About the Role: This is a cross-border position supporting offices in France, Germany and the Middle East. The successful candidate will report to the Senior Manager, Compliance Programs, and work closely with local Compliance Partners and MLROs.


What You’ll Bring:

  • Fluency in French and English (additional proficiency in German and/or Arabic is a plus)
  • Solid understanding of AML regulations, trade sanctions, and export control compliance
  • Experience in client onboarding and compliance within a professional services or law firm environment
  • Strong research, analytical, and communication skills
  • Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced, international setting
  • Familiarity with tools such as World-Check, Compliance Catalyst, Intapp, and Microsoft 365


This is a fantastic opportunity to join a globally respected firm and contribute to the development of robust compliance processes across multiple jurisdictions.



Due to the high volume of applications, we are not able to respond to all enquiries. If you have not received a response within 72 hours, please assume you have not been shortlisted at this stage, however thank you for taking the time to apply.


Ryder Reid Legal is a recruitment specialist. For over thirty years we've been connecting legal talent with many of the leading law firms in London and internationally. Follow our LinkedIn page for the latest vacancies.


https://uk.linkedin.com/company/ryder-reid-legal

descriptionHtml:
About the Job

Are you ready to take the lead on regulatory compliance at a global investment organisation? This is a high-profile, hands-on opportunity for a senior compliance leader to own the strategy, shape the frameworks, and deliver real impact. If you're looking for a role where you can get your teeth into complex challenges and influence outcomes at the highest level, this is it.


The Client

Our client is a dynamic, purpose-driven financial institution operating across multiple international markets, including a strong presence in emerging economies. With a mission to enable sustainable growth and responsible investment, the organisation works on high-value, cross-border transactions in some of the world’s most exciting markets. As an FCA-regulated entity, it faces sophisticated compliance challenges requiring strategic leadership and operational excellence.


The Role

This role places you at the heart of compliance leadership, overseeing the full spectrum of regulatory obligations while enabling the business to grow globally. You will have direct exposure to senior executives and the Board, owning the organisation’s approach to regulatory and financial crime compliance across diverse jurisdictions. Key responsibilities include:

  • Lead the compliance strategy end-to-end – define, implement, and evolve frameworks across multiple regions and emerging markets.
  • Take ownership of SMF16 & SMF17 responsibilities – you must already hold these functions or demonstrate the capability and ambition to step up.
  • Oversee AML, CTF, sanctions, anti-bribery, and financial crime controls with precision and authority.
  • Manage a skilled compliance team, coaching and developing talent while driving a proactive compliance culture.
  • Ensure the business meets its FCA and international obligations while enabling innovation and responsible growth.
  • Deliver insightful MI reporting to the Board and senior leadership, influencing strategy at the highest level.
  • Collaborate closely with investment, legal, risk, and operational teams on high-value, cross-border initiatives.
  • Stay ahead of regulatory changes and emerging risks, ensuring the organisation’s frameworks evolve with the market.


About You

This role requires a strategic operator with the drive, curiosity, and courage to make an impact. You’ll be someone who thrives in fast-paced environments and wants to immerse yourself in the detail while shaping the bigger picture.

  • Proven SMF16 & SMF17 experience (or demonstrable readiness to step up into these controlled functions).
  • Extensive expertise in regulatory compliance within financial services, ideally across private equity, asset management, or investment banking.
  • Strong international exposure with hands-on experience navigating regulatory frameworks in emerging markets.
  • Deep knowledge of FCA requirements, UK Money Laundering Regulations 2017, JMLSG Guidance, and financial crime frameworks.
  • Experience building, scaling, and leading compliance frameworks across multiple jurisdictions.
  • Strong leadership and team management experience, empowering others while driving accountability.
  • A hands-on, sleeves-rolled-up attitude – this role is perfect for someone who wants to get involved and make a visible impact.
  • Exceptional communication, negotiation, and influencing skills at all levels.


What’s on Offer?

  • A pivotal leadership role within a purpose-driven, globally operating financial institution.
  • Competitive salary, bonus, and comprehensive benefits package.
  • The opportunity to own SMF16 & SMF17 responsibilities and shape strategy at Board level.
  • Hands-on exposure to complex, cross-border transactions and emerging markets.
  • A collaborative, ambitious, and fast-paced environment designed to stretch and challenge you.


For more information contact Luke Masih (luke.masih@thesjp.co.uk)

descriptionHtml:
The Role


Legal Executive Assistant - Corporate - Up to £37,000 salary


A leading international law firm is seeking an experienced executive assistant to join its high-performing corporate team in the Leeds office. This is an excellent opportunity for a proactive and organised individual who thrives in a fast-paced, professional services environment.


You'll work closely with senior lawyers, providing day-to-day support across diary and travel management, client coordination, and team operations. The role offers great variety and scope for someone looking to be an integral part of a dynamic, collaborative team.


Key responsibilities


  • Manage complex diaries and coordinate international travel and meetings
  • Prepare documents, meeting agendas, and follow up on action points
  • Support client engagement, business development activities, and pitch preparation
  • Maintain well-organised and compliant filing systems using iManage
  • Assist with onboarding, compliance, billing, and financial reporting using Aderant
  • Liaise with clients and internal teams, ensuring clear, professional communication
  • Draft correspondence, manage shared inboxes, and coordinate with other departments
  • Take ownership of administrative processes and contribute to team efficiency


What you'll bring


  • Previous experience as an executive assistant or legal PA, ideally in a law firm or professional services setting
  • Strong organisational and multitasking skills, with excellent attention to detail
  • Confidence managing busy schedules and coordinating across time zones
  • Strong communication skills and the ability to build rapport at all levels
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office; experience with iManage and Aderant is an advantage
  • A proactive, flexible approach and ability to stay ahead of what's needed


What's on offer


  • Join a collaborative, well-respected team in the Leeds office
  • Hybrid working model with a strong focus on wellbeing and flexibility
  • Genuine opportunities for career development and progression
  • A people-first, inclusive culture where your contribution is valued


If you're a confident, capable EA looking for your next challenge in a top-tier corporate team, apply now to find out more.


Apply in the strictest of confidence online and or via telephone. There is no need for a CV for an initial conversation.


Contact: Tara Halliwell (Retained Consultant)

Direct Dial: 0161 826 3140 Mobile: 0793 999 2563

Email: Tara.Halliwell@williamjamesrecruitment.com

London - 3 days a week in office

£70,000 - £90,000 plus package


Middlemore are currently working with a mid-sized, international financial services firm to hire an Employee Relations, HR Governance & Compliance Manager. This is a newly created role, reporting to the HR Director, taking ownership of complex employee relations activity, whilst also leading HR's regulatory, compliance, policy and people risk governance agenda.


You will be the Employee Relations and HR compliance subject matter expert across the EMEA region, providing high-level advisory support on complex employee matters while ensuring policies and practices align with both regulatory expectations and evolving employment legislation. We're proudly part of the Simplyhealth family, one of the biggest names in UK healthcare.
companySlogan: Seamless payment experiences for you and your patients.
descriptionHtml:
Who is Practi?

Practi is building the FinTech for UK Healthcare. At a time when the healthcare landscape in the UK is changing rapidly, we're building solutions for both healthcare professionals, and patients, that increase access to healthcare for everyone.

54% of patients who attend a private medical clinic for an assessment consultation, then decide not to move forward, and our research tells us this either due to patient anxiety and/or affordability of the treatment.

Practi has built a platform that allows clinicians to give their patients a premium experience with educational treatment content to ease anxiety about moving forwards, with an embedded finance solution that allows patients to apply for finance to eliminate affordability as a barrier to receiving healthcare.

Practi is a corporate venture, proudly part of the Simplyhealth group (a purpose-driven B Corp), which means we have all the financial stability and industry expertise or an established player, but with the agility and autonomy of a start-up.

We're passionate about solving these issues for everyone, and whilst we've started in the Dental sector, we have big plans to expand across multiple healthcare verticals. We’ve got a world-class leadership team onboard, led by a successful serial entrepreneur, and we're actively building out the team as we begin to scale - come join us!

About The Role

We are looking for a detail-oriented and organised Operations Associate to join our rapidly growing team. In this role you’ll be instrumental in ensuring the smooth operation of our FinTech for Healthcare platform, from customer onboarding and support, to billing and process improvement. You’ll collaborate across teams to enhance workflows, solve operational challenges, and drive efficiency.

As an early-stage company, we value adaptability. You’ll play a key role in building and refining our processes—whether improving existing workflows or creating new ones from scratch. This is a varied role that is growing and evolving as quickly as our business. Every day will bring new challenges, offering a great opportunity to make a real impact and grow with us!

Requirements

Key Responsibilities

  • Customer Onboarding: Ensure a seamless onboarding experience by coordinating with partners and internal teams across the business to meet all requirements efficiently.
  • Process Development: Identify pain points, share insights, and recommend improvements to streamline business operations.
  • Customer Support: Be the first point of contact for customer inquiries, providing support via phone and chat to help them get the most out of our platform.
  • Regulatory Compliance: Work with the compliance team to ensure all operational processes meet regulatory requirements.
  • Data Management: Handle and process sensitive data with accuracy and efficiency.
  • Financial Operations: Assist with payments, invoicing, financial reporting, and audits.

Who You Are

  • Organised: Skilled at managing and prioritising multiple pieces of work effectively.
  • Proactive: Self-starter with a strong bias for action and getting things done at pace.
  • Attention to Detail: Highly accurate and precise, with a keen eye for spotting inconsistencies and discrepancies.
  • Excellent Communicator: Strong verbal and written communication skills for engaging with customers and collaborating with colleagues.
  • Fast Learner: Quickly grasps new concepts, tools, and processes; comfortable navigating ambiguity to resolve challenges.
  • Curious & Data-Driven: Enjoys digging into data, spotting trends, and using insights to improve processes and decision-making.
  • Team Player: Works well with others and thrives in a collaborative environment.
  • Eligibility to Work in the UK: Must have the legal right to work in the United Kingdom (e.g., Citizenship, ILR, valid work visa).
  • No Need to Tick Every Box: If you’re excited about this role but don’t meet 100% of the requirements, we’d still love to hear from you! We value potential and a willingness to learn.

Desirable Skills & Experience

  • Previous experience in an operations role at a startup or fast-paced environment, preferably in healthcare, finance, or SaaS
  • Tech-savvy and comfortable using a range of tools; experience with Intercom, Stripe, HubSpot or Airtable is a plus.

Working Pattern

  • 3 days a week in our office (WorkingFrom Hoxton, 32 Blackfriars Road, Southwark, SE1 8DP)

Benefits

Benefits

  • Up to 10% in Employer Pension Contribution
  • Health Cash Plan
  • 4x Life Insurance
  • 28 Days Holiday (36 including Bank Holidays)
  • £600 Health & Wellbeing budget
  • Quarterly Team Away Days
  • Flexible Working

The Importance we Place on Equity, Diversity & Inclusion

  • Building a company to serve clinicians and patients in healthcare means we need to maximise our diversity of thought, so we can bring the best solutions to bear on such important problems. We also know that diversity of thought is a product of diverse talent, which manifests itself in many ways.
  • Gender, sexual orientation, ethnicity, neurodiversity and socio-economic background are all dimensions that we care about and we encourage applications from typically under-represented groups.

Our Values at Practi

  • Courage: Choosing bravery over comfort, we stand firm in our values and tackle difficult tasks head-on. We lean into challenges, knowing that progress require resilience and bold action.
  • Trust: We count on each other by fostering an environment of transparency, integrity, and accountability. With ownership of our actions, we create a foundation of reliability, allowing us to operate with autonomy while supporting each other when needed.
  • ‍♀️ Ambition: Unapologetically bold, we push ourselves to achieve more, constantly striving for excellence. We drive progress with pace, always questioning the status quo and finding new ways to make a meaningful impact for both our team and our customers.
  • Play: We believe in taking our work, but not ourselves, seriously. By bringing, levity, and joy to our day-to-day, we create a positive atmosphere where we can do our best work, celebrate successes, and build meaningful relationships with each other.
  • ❤️ Humanity: Empathy, compassion, and inclusion are at the heart of how we operate. We bring our whole selves to work, creating a supportive and safe environment where everyone feels valued. By practicing active listening and kindness, we strengthen our team and our culture.
  • Curiosity: We are committed to continuous learning, always seeking new knowledge and growth opportunities. With humility, we approach each day with a hunger to improve, knowing that small, consistent steps lead to transformative results over time.

descriptionHtml:
Compliance Business Partner - Financial Services, AML

FTSE 100 business

London, 2 days a week in the office

£70,000 - £80,000 per annum DOE + 10% bonus


Audit & Risk Recruitment are working with a FTSE 100 business in the telecommunications sector. This business has a selection of financial services products (money transfers, mobile payments) which require specialist regulatory support from a legal compliance perspective. Our client is in need of technical expertise in the anti-money laundering (AML) space. This is a great opportunity for someone looking to leave financial services to work in the telco/tech sector.


Key responsibilities will include:

  • Strategic partnership with members of the senior leadership team, ensuring compliance with regulations and providing expertise aimed at mitigating risks surrounding financial crime, particularly anti-money laundering (AML)
  • Working with regional markets to assess their risk mitigation strategies and project managing remediations and improvements to the compliance programs.
  • Driving the implementation of group-wide compliance policies and programs to regional markets, working as the go-between for Group and Regions.
  • Delivering tailored training programmes on financial crime and compliance.
  • Regulatory reporting


The ideal candidate for this role will be an established Compliance Manager with a keen understanding of financial crime in regulated sectors (banking, insurance for example). You'll be an analytic thinker and problem solver who can digest complex information and make it understandable for others.
